## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Next: cloud-build-reliquary-preflight (₢BBAA3) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  Defense-in-depth — every Cloud Build validates reliquary GAR-presence from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
  
  ## Motivation
  The host-side preflight (`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`) verifies the Director's host can reach GAR. It does NOT verify what actually matters at build time: the worker pool's view of GAR. Three real divergence sources — airgap pool private VPC routing, IAM split between Director (host) and Mason (build) SAs, and time skew between submission and execution.
  
  ## Surface
  - New step script (rbgjr* or analogous) — gcloud/alpine HEAD-checks against reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors
  - Step-assembly prepend: conjure (`rbfd_build` stitch), bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`), graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`), enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`)
  - Specs: RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE — declare the in-pool preflight step
  - RBS0:800 (cloud-build-step contexts paragraph) — extend to mention in-pool preflight contract
  
  ## Chicken-and-egg note
  The preflight step's own image is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Preflight covers the other five tool images plus enshrinements. Document the boundary explicitly in spec.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  - Reliquary universalization (this heat's prior pace) lands first.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  - Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path Cloud Build job.
  - Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) produces a clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message.
  - Green case overhead acceptable (~5s).
  
  ## Out of scope
  - Host-side preflight tightening — covered by prior pace.
  - BBAAs empirical run.


Recommended: `jjx_arm` ₢₢BBAA3 or mount BB

4e7b5f29  ₢BBAAs    enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). Block comment + docstring updated to remove skip-if-set framing. Commit-message string in source: removed coronet prefix from new commit string per directive (coronets should not appear in source). Pre-existing coronet-prefixed commit strings elsewhere in this file (BBAA0/BBAAu) are left as-is; broader sweep is follow-up work. This notch parallels f365fe89's revert (03a2a17a) — net effect from BBAA0 baseline: BBAA0's enshrine-overload pattern reverted, orchestration's pin-validation step removed, spec/comments drift TBD.                                                                                                                                                                                                         
da698def  ₢BBAAs    BBAAs revert f365fe89 (enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate). The four-way classifier patch added more branching to a switch that exists only because BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be both 'mirror upstream + write back' (forge case) and 'HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark' (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch is wrong-direction; the right move is to remove the overload. Operator-driven reframing: the classifier's HEAD-200 'skip' path is not an optimization — it actively breaks enshrine's contract. Enshrine's job is to compute the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream; HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest forever, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's commit 14242011 records empirically: 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac when upstream digest moved). The next notch implements the simplification: drop the entire classifier, make rbfd_enshrine always submit the CB job, remove the orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call (the hallmark-pin's existence is established by ordain-forge's success, not by a separate validation step), partial-revert BBAA0 spec prose accordingly. This notch restores rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh and RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc to their pre-f365fe89 state via git revert --no-commit. bash -n green; theurge build green (no Rust changes). b4062af8  ₢BBAAs    BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated. Surfaced empirically in BBAAs onboarding-sequence run on a fresh canonical-established depot — the committed forge ANCHOR rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac was depot-stale (no enshrinement in the new GAR), and the classifier fatal-died before the regenerable CB path could run. Fix branches the 404 case on ORIGIN: with ORIGIN set, write 'enshrine' classification (extract step overwrites the stale value with the freshly-computed locator); with ORIGIN empty, keep the existing fatal die. Pipeline downstream of the classifier needs no changes — submit already passes ORIGIN substitutions only for to-enshrine slots, and extract already overwrites existing ANCHOR lines via in-place line replacement. Spec sweep: RBSAE head NOTE rewritten to describe the four-way semantics (skip / regenerate / fatal-pin / fresh-enshrine / empty); Classify Slots step expanded from 'pre-populated vs to-enshrine' to the explicit 404+ORIGIN-set branch; Extract step's 'pre-populated slots not touched' updated to 'skip slots not touched, stale-cache reclassifications written through'. BCG audit: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, mutable locals for per-iteration synthesized values (Exception 2), comment justifying non-obvious WHY (asymmetric ORIGIN-set vs ORIGIN-empty handling on 404), continue inside for loop, no new bash 4+ features, no heredocs. Verification: bash -n green, theurge build green, 0/0 unit tests pass, fast suite 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA0/BBAA2/BBAAz baseline). Next: cloud-build-reliquary-preflight (₢BBAA3) [rough]

Docket:
  ## Character
  Defense-in-depth — every Cloud Build validates reliquary GAR-presence from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
  
  ## Motivation
  The host-side preflight (`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`) verifies the Director's host can reach GAR. It does NOT verify what actually matters at build time: the worker pool's view of GAR. Three real divergence sources — airgap pool private VPC routing, IAM split between Director (host) and Mason (build) SAs, and time skew between submission and execution.
  
  ## Surface
  - New step script (rbgjr* or analogous) — gcloud/alpine HEAD-checks against reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors
  - Step-assembly prepend: conjure (`rbfd_build` stitch), bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`), graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`), enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`)
  - Specs: RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE — declare the in-pool preflight step
  - RBS0:800 (cloud-build-step contexts paragraph) — extend to mention in-pool preflight contract
  
  ## Chicken-and-egg note
  The preflight step's own image is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Preflight covers the other five tool images plus enshrinements. Document the boundary explicitly in spec.
  
  ## Cross-pace dependencies
  - Reliquary universalization (this heat's prior pace) lands first.
  
  ## Wrap criterion
  - Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path Cloud Build job.
  - Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) produces a clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message.
  - Green case overhead acceptable (~5s).
  
  ## Out of scope
  - Host-side preflight tightening — covered by prior pace.
  - BBAAs empirical run.

4e7b5f29  ₢BBAAs    enshrine simplify: remove BBAA0 classifier overload, drop orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call. The classifier conflated two qualitatively different jobs into a single tabtarget: (1) mirror upstream + write back ANCHOR (forge case), (2) HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch was wrong-direction; the right move was to remove the overload so each job has a clean home. The HEAD-200 'skip' path was framed as an optimization but actively breaks enshrine's contract: HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's earlier commit 14242011 records empirically when rust:slim-bookworm digest moved 5ae2d2ef98 to caaf9ca7ac). rbfd_enshrine now: (a) requires at least one slot has RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN populated (ANCHOR-only is no longer a valid input shape; ANCHOR is a derived artifact of this operation, not a pin); (b) unconditionally submits the Cloud Build job that inspects current upstream, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR; (c) extract overwrites whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR. Re-runs are idempotent w.r.t. upstream state, not w.r.t. the file's prior contents — that distinction was the original design mistake. Internal pipeline: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots removed entirely (84 lines), zrbfd_enshrine_submit's classification-file read loop replaced with direct ${RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN:-} reads. Empty ORIGIN slots become empty CB substitutions; the CB step's existing `test -n ${ORIGIN} || continue` guard skips them naturally. Orchestration: rbtdro_onboarding.rs drops the enshrine-airgap-validate call after the airgap-bottle anchor write; the forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by ordain-forge's success above, no separate validation step needed. airgap_sigil local removed (now unused). These are documentation drift, deferred to a follow-up notch per operator pause. BCG audit on edited bash: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, one declaration per line, mutable locals for synthesized values (Exception 2), no bash 4+ features, no heredocs, no inline shellcheck. Verification: bash -n green on rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh; theurge build green (rbtdro_onboarding.rs compiles); empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount. This notch parallels f365fe89's revert (03a2a17a) — net effect from BBAA0 baseline: BBAA0's enshrine-overload pattern reverted, orchestration's pin-validation step removed, spec/comments drift TBD.                                                                                                                                                                                                         
da698def  ₢BBAAs    BBAAs revert f365fe89 (enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate). The four-way classifier patch added more branching to a switch that exists only because BBAA0 overloaded enshrine to be both 'mirror upstream + write back' (forge case) and 'HEAD-validate orchestration-pinned hallmark' (airgap-bottle case). Refining the switch is wrong-direction; the right move is to remove the overload. Operator-driven reframing: the classifier's HEAD-200 'skip' path is not an optimization — it actively breaks enshrine's contract. Enshrine's job is to compute the current correct ANCHOR for the current upstream; HEAD-200 on a cached locator proves only that some manifest exists at that path, not that it matches current upstream. Skipping based on cache reachability silently locks the vessel to a stale digest forever, hiding the upstream-rotated case (the case Brad's commit 14242011 records empirically: 5ae2d2ef98 → caaf9ca7ac when upstream digest moved). The next notch implements the simplification: drop the entire classifier, make rbfd_enshrine always submit the CB job, remove the orchestration's enshrine-airgap-validate call (the hallmark-pin's existence is established by ordain-forge's success, not by a separate validation step), partial-revert BBAA0 spec prose accordingly. This notch restores rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh and RBSAE-ark_enshrine.adoc to their pre-f365fe89 state via git revert --no-commit. bash -n green; theurge build green (no Rust changes).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
b4062af8  ₢BBAAs    BBAAs enshrine-classifier-stale-cache-regenerate: zrbfd_enshrine_classify_slots conflated two qualitatively different cases when RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR was populated and HEAD returned 404 — orchestration-written hallmark-pin (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN empty) and committed-derived cache (ANCHOR set, ORIGIN set, e.g. forge vessel's rust:slim-bookworm enshrinement). BBAA0's skip-if-set design fail-fasted on both with 'caller wrote an unresolvable locator', which is correct for the pin case but wrong for the cache case: ORIGIN is the source of truth and the stale ANCHOR is a derived value that can be regenerated. Surfaced empirically in BBAAs onboarding-sequence run on a fresh canonical-established depot — the committed forge ANCHOR rbi_es/rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac:rust-slim-bookworm-caaf9ca7ac was depot-stale (no enshrinement in the new GAR), and the classifier fatal-died before the regenerable CB path could run. Fix branches the 404 case on ORIGIN: with ORIGIN set, write 'enshrine' classification (extract step overwrites the stale value with the freshly-computed locator); with ORIGIN empty, keep the existing fatal die. Pipeline downstream of the classifier needs no changes — submit already passes ORIGIN substitutions only for to-enshrine slots, and extract already overwrites existing ANCHOR lines via in-place line replacement. Spec sweep: RBSAE head NOTE rewritten to describe the four-way semantics (skip / regenerate / fatal-pin / fresh-enshrine / empty); Classify Slots step expanded from 'pre-populated vs to-enshrine' to the explicit 404+ORIGIN-set branch; Extract step's 'pre-populated slots not touched' updated to 'skip slots not touched, stale-cache reclassifications written through'. BCG audit: test command over [[ ]], braced quoted expansion, || buc_die on every I/O, mutable locals for per-iteration synthesized values (Exception 2), comment justifying non-obvious WHY (asymmetric ORIGIN-set vs ORIGIN-empty handling on 404), continue inside for loop, no new bash 4+ features, no heredocs. Verification: bash -n green, theurge build green, 0/0 unit tests pass, fast suite 25/26 (rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels pre-existing fail unchanged from BBAA0/BBAA2/BBAAz baseline). Empirical re-run of ordain-airgap deferred per docket — operator drives next mount.

content.content:
1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BB
2	
3	## Context
4	
5	Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.
6	
7	This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.
8	
9	## Single operator prerequisite chain
10	
11	Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.
12	
13	## Entry contract — load-bearing
14	
15	`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:
16	
17	- Working tree clean
18	- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
19	- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
20	- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
21	- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
22	- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files
23	
24	Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.
25	
26	## Failure mode contract
27	
28	Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.
29	
30	## Release-branch execution contract
31	
32	Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.
33	
34	**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.
35	
36	**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.
37	
38	**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.
39	
40	Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.
41	
42	## Tier layering
43	
44	Three tiers, escalating cost:
45	
46	| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
47	|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
48	| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
49	| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
50	| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |
51	
52	`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.
53	
54	## Gauntlet test suite — design
55	
56	`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.
57	
58	**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.
59	
60	**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).
61	
62	**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:
63	
64	1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
65	2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
66	3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
67	4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
68	5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
69	6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
70	7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)
71	
72	No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.
73	
74	## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage
75	
76	The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.
77	
78	Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.
79	
80	**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.
81	
82	Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.
83	
84	Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.
85	
86	## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing
87	
88	Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:
89	
90	- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
91	- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.
92	
93	**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.
94	
95	Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.
96	
97	**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.
98	
99	## Suite-level fail-fast
100	
101	Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.
102	
103	## No-teardown decision
104	
105	The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:
106	
107	- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
108	- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
109	- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
110	- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.
111	
112	Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).
113	
114	## Operator scope
115	
116	Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.
117	
118	## Coupling to ₣A_
119	
120	Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.
121	
122	## Locked design decisions
123	
124	- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
125	- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
126	- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.
127	
128	## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh
129	
130	Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.
131	
132	**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.
133	
134	**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):
135	
136	- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
137	- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
138	- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
139	- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.
140	
141	Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.
142	
143	Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.
144	
145	## References
146	
147	- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
148	- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
149	- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced

151	- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

Three real divergence sources — airgap pool private VPC routing, IAM split between Director (host) and Mason (build) SAs, and time skew between submission and execution.

163	- Step-assembly prepend: conjure (`rbfd_build` stitch), bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`), graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`), enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`)

# jjezs_paddock ₣BB

## Context

Release qualification gap surfaced by the ₣A_ depot-regen heat. Every existing test tier (`rbw-tf` fast, `rbw-tr` release) tolerates accumulated depot state, so silent first-build assumptions slip through. Recent spooks — kludge-aware-charge-prereq (k-prefixed hallmarks at GAR), ZRBOB_PROJECT (compose project name without runtime prefix), rbob_charged_predicate prefix omission, reliquary integrity-broken on negative test — all share a property: they only manifest on first-build paths, and none were caught until live verification surfaced them.

This heat constructs `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third tier that **refuses to run** unless marshal-zero state was just committed. The refusal is enforced by the test itself, not by ceremony or operator discipline.

## Single operator prerequisite chain

Operator has exactly one prerequisite chain: confirm Payor health → marshal-zero → commit → run `rbw-tP`. Payor OAuth is the only credential the operator must have ready; everything else (governor, retriever, director SAs and their RBRA files) is **minted by the qualification itself**, not restored from backup.

## Entry contract — load-bearing

`rbw-tP` fails-fast unless ALL of the following hold:

- Working tree clean
- HEAD commit is a marshal-zero commit (detectable signature baked into `rblm_zero` per BBAAB)
- RBRR fields are blank (prefixes, depot project ID, etc.)
- No RBRA credential files present (governor, retriever, director — all deleted by marshal-zero, recreated by the qualification)
- No hallmark fields populated in nameplates
- No depot-scoped fields populated in vessel rbrv.env files

Operator cannot skip the prerequisite. This is the property that makes the tier catch the silent-first-build bug class by construction.

## Failure mode contract

Mid-qualification failure means start-over-from-zero, not patch-and-continue. Documented explicitly in runbook to prevent the very accumulated-state bug class this tier exists to catch.

## Release-branch execution contract

Three properties bind every fixture and phase in this tier.

**Run on a release branch.** The release machinery is engineered for branch execution, not main. The branch will accumulate many commits across a successful pristine run. After a failed run, the branch holds bygone commits that `rbw-MZ` does not touch — they remain as history; the next run starts from a fresh marshal-zero commit on top.

**Commits during the run are first-class.** Each step that mutates regime state (rbrr.env, rbra files, vessel rbrv.env, etc.) commits the change. Container-recipe work has a documented cognitive failure mode: humans lose track of where they are in long sequences. The growing commit trail is the operator's mental anchor against that.

**Stop on very first failure, cleanly and clearly.** A step fails → fixture stops → operator stops → debugging happens immediately on the failed branch. Stderr names the failed step; fixture exits non-zero. Recovery is `rbw-MZ` + retry on a fresh marshal-zero. No graceful degradation, no recovery branches.

Engineering scaffolding for any other shape — partial-run cleanup, soft-delete tolerance, multi-mode failure handling, recovery-time orphan inspection, diagnostic-redundancy — is non-load-bearing and does not belong in pristine-tier code. The single mechanical path is the entire surface.

## Tier layering

Three tiers, escalating cost:

| Colophon | Frontispiece | Cost | Entry contract |
|----------|--------------|------|----------------|
| `rbw-tf` | QualifyFast | seconds | none |
| `rbw-tr` | QualifyRelease | minutes | none (accumulated state OK) |
| `rbw-tP` | QualifyPristine *(new)* | ~1 hour + cloud $ | marshal-zero just committed |

`rbw-tP` is THE release gate. `rbw-tr` becomes the pre-pristine smoke test — cheap-and-frequent for development confidence; pristine for actual release.

## Gauntlet test suite — design

`rbw-tP` exec's the **gauntlet suite** — a TestSuite (not a custom orchestrator script) composed of fixtures sequenced from marshal-zero state through canonical-credentialed state to crucible verification. The TestSuite mechanism is existing infrastructure (`tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.{fast,service,crucible,complete}.sh`); the gauntlet suite extends it.

**Suite-of-fixtures, not orchestrator-script.** The original construction plan (custom bash orchestrator with inline tabtarget calls) was abandoned in favor of suite composition. Each section of the release-qualification sequence becomes a fixture; the suite composes them in order.

**Naming hygiene.** "Gauntlet" is the suite name; "pristine" is reserved for the §1 pristine-lifecycle fixture and informally for tier vocabulary. The suite name does not appear at fixture or case level — distinct stratification across naming layers (suite ≠ fixture ≠ case).

**State ladder, fixtures along it.** The suite walks a state ladder no single fixture covers:

1. enrollment-validation (preflight, state-indifferent)
2. pristine-lifecycle (§1: marshal-zero gate + throwaway depot/SA lifecycle)
3. canonical-establish (§2: canonical depot levy + governor mantle + retriever invest + director invest + IAM propagation wait)
4. canonical-onboarding-sequence (§3: reliquary inscribe + per-vessel hallmark ordain — handbook-driven build walks per BBAAf)
5. regime-validation, regime-smoke (post-§3, regimes now populated)
6. four-mode (cloud-build mode coverage)
7. tadmor, moriah, srjcl, pluml (§4: per-crucible test fixtures, vessel-exists preconditions per BBAAs)

No automatic teardown — see "No-teardown decision" below.

## Dual purpose: handbook-regression coverage

The gauntlet's primary purpose is release qualification (catch silent first-build assumptions). Its **secondary purpose** is automated handbook-regression coverage: ensure operator-facing onboarding handbook tracks (`rbh*`) stay in sync with working code so future operators following the prescribed steps aren't surprised by drift.

Build-side handbook coverage lives in BBAAf (canonical-onboarding-sequence): each ordain case walks the corresponding director-mode handbook track (`rbw-Odf` conjure / `rbw-Oda` airgap / `rbw-Odb` bind / `rbw-Odg` graft) rather than calling inscribe/ordain machinery directly.

**Build/run split — load-bearing.** Handbook walks cover the BUILD step (vessel construction) and stop when the hallmark lands in GAR. Run-side fixtures (per-crucible: tadmor/moriah/srjcl/pluml) stay as direct test fixtures with vessel-exists preconditions, NOT handbook walks. The split keeps run-side reusable against any vessel source — coupling them to handbook prose breaks reusability.

Drift between fixtures and handbooks is accepted future maintenance: whoever changes one is responsible for updating the other; the gauntlet's job is to surface drift, not prevent it by construction.

Run-side handbook coverage (`rbw-Ots`, the rack portion of `rbw-Ofc`) is explicitly out of scope for this heat.

## Fixture disposition — Independent vs StateProgressing

Each fixture declares a disposition flag in Rust, controlling engine behavior:

- **Independent** — cases are self-contained; suite-order is informational. Engine permits keep-going mode for surveying.
- **StateProgressing** — case N's success establishes preconditions for case N+1. Engine refuses keep-going mode (incoherent: failed case leaves broken precondition). Fail-fast required.

**Default is Independent.** Existing fixtures (`enrollment-validation`, `regime-validation`, `regime-smoke`, `four-mode`, `tadmor`, `moriah`, `srjcl`, `pluml`) are Independent and require no tagging change. Only fixtures whose case-N success establishes case-N+1 preconditions get StateProgressing — currently `pristine-lifecycle`, `canonical-establish`, and `canonical-onboarding-sequence`.

Suite-level disposition derives from membership: any StateProgressing fixture in the suite makes the suite state-progressing → suite-level keep-going refused.

**Per-case precondition probes.** Each case in a StateProgressing fixture probes its precondition at start. If state matches expectation, run. If not, refuse with operator-actionable diagnostic naming the expected state and the closest fixture that produces it. This enables safe a-la-carte single-case rerun — the engine doesn't need to know the case's history; the case's probe enforces.

## Suite-level fail-fast

Across all suites — not just gauntlet — any fixture failure stops subsequent fixtures from running automatically. State-progressing suites require this for correctness; independent suites adopt it for the simpler mental model. Operators who want full surveying run individual fixtures separately.

## No-teardown decision

The gauntlet suite omits an automatic teardown phase. After green tally, the canonical depot, SAs, hallmarks, and RBRA files persist for operator inspection. Deliberate trade-off:

- **Inspect-after-success** is genuine operator value — browse canonical depot in GCP console, inspect GAR contents, run ad-hoc verification a test sequence cannot anticipate.
- **Cleanup is operator-driven** — manual sequence is `rbw-fA` per vessel + `rbw-arD` + `rbw-adD` + `rbw-dU` (governor cleanup folds into unmake per BBAAN). All existing tabtargets; no new infrastructure.
- **Failed runs don't accumulate cleanup debt** — under fail-fast, teardown wouldn't run anyway; the no-teardown decision only changes behavior on full success.
- **Cost visibility** — pending-delete soft-deleted projects accumulate per run (RELEASE.md accepts this); not changed by teardown decision.

Documentation of the post-success cleanup ceremony is BBAAI work (RELEASE.md).

## Operator scope

Single operator (project lead). Not designed for multi-operator workflow. The README.md release section remains the runbook for the gauntlet itself; the dual-purpose handbook-regression coverage protects the broader operator surface (onboarding tracks under `rbh*`) from drift.

## Coupling to ₣A_

Cutover work from ₣A_ informing `rbw-tP`'s sequence has landed (BBAAM depot-identity-collapse). ₣A_'s remaining paces run independently of BB.

## Locked design decisions

- **Depot identity collapse**: RBRR collapses to `RBRR_DEPOT_MONIKER`; `RBRR_CLOUD_PREFIX` flows through depot-affiliated resources; per-moniker depot fact-files; pristine-fixture moniker autodetect lives in Rust (`rbtdrp_pristine.rs`), not payor.
- **Cult-verb naming**: SA domain muster→**roster**; image domain muster→**audit**. Domain-exclusive split; lowercase tail letters preserved for no-cloud-change observation colophons.
- **Build/run split**: handbook walks cover build only; per-crucible fixtures stay reusable as direct tests.

## Calibrant fixture family — BBAAh

Synthetic Rust fixtures with deterministic verdicts, registered in the manifest by BBAAh. The family exists to give ₣BC `rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test` deterministic targets for bash-blackbox driving and manifest-coverage validation; here in ₣BB only the foundation lands.

**Anchor word: `calibrant`.** Module `rbtdrl_calibrant.rs` (classifier `l` for caLibrant since `c` is owned by crucible). Replaces the dormant `rbtdrd_dummy.rs` placeholder. Test counterpart `rbtdtl_calibrant.rs`. Internal-only vocabulary; not end-user-facing.

**Fixture catalog** (Rust, manifest-registered):

- `calibrant-verdicts` (Independent) — pass / fail / skip / pass_with_output. Verdict-path coverage. The case-written file (`output.txt`) is distinct from the engine-auto `trace.txt`.
- `calibrant-fail-fast` (Independent, multi-section) — fixture-internal fail-fast across cases and across sections; "not_reached" cases write sentinel files used as absence-of-side-effect assertions.
- `calibrant-progressing` (StateProgressing) — exercises `rbtdrb_Probe` diagnostic format and `rbtdre_resolve_fail_fast` policy gate.
- `calibrant-sentinel` (Independent) — single case writing a known file when it runs. Used for suite-level fail-fast verification.

Suite: `calibrant` = [verdicts, fail-fast, sentinel]. Sentinel-after-fail verifies cross-fixture fail-fast contract.

Downstream consumption — bash testbench, manifest-coverage runtime checks, coverage-validation fixtures and driver — lives in ₣BC.

## References

- ₣A_ rbk-mvp-3-resource-prefix-and-depot-regen — surfaced the gap
- ₣BC rbk-mvp-3-theurge-self-test — calibrant downstream consumers
- `tt/rbw-tr.QualifyRelease.sh` — current release qualify; layered alongside, not replaced
- `Tools/rbk/rblm_cli.sh` — `rbw-MZ` zeroes local regime; marshal-zero signature baked here per BBAAB
- `.claude/commands/rbk-prep-release.md` — upstream contribution ceremony; pristine-pass becomes a precondition (BBAAH)

# jjezs_pace ₢BBAA3

## Character
Defense-in-depth — every Cloud Build validates reliquary GAR-presence from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.

## Motivation
The host-side preflight (`zrbfd_preflight_reliquary`) verifies the Director's host can reach GAR. It does NOT verify what actually matters at build time: the worker pool's view of GAR. Three real divergence sources — airgap pool private VPC routing, IAM split between Director (host) and Mason (build) SAs, and time skew between submission and execution.

## Surface
- New step script (rbgjr* or analogous) — gcloud/alpine HEAD-checks against reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors
- Step-assembly prepend: conjure (`rbfd_build` stitch), bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`), graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`), enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`)
- Specs: RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE — declare the in-pool preflight step
- RBS0:800 (cloud-build-step contexts paragraph) — extend to mention in-pool preflight contract

## Chicken-and-egg note
The preflight step's own image is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Preflight covers the other five tool images plus enshrinements. Document the boundary explicitly in spec.

## Cross-pace dependencies
- Reliquary universalization (this heat's prior pace) lands first.

## Wrap criterion
- Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path Cloud Build job.
- Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) produces a clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message.
- Green case overhead acceptable (~5s).

## Out of scope
- Host-side preflight tightening — covered by prior pace.
- BBAAs empirical run.

content.text:
## Heat ₣BB — rbk-mvp-3-release-qualification

**Progress**: 33/47 done, racing.

**Paddock shape**: Building `rbw-tP` QualifyPristine — a third release tier that refuses to run unless marshal-zero state was just committed. Architecture: gauntlet test suite composed of state-laddered fixtures (Independent vs StateProgressing). The big new thing this heat exists to catch: silent first-build assumptions that current `rbw-tf`/`rbw-tr` tiers tolerate.

**Recent landing (₢BBAA4)**: Reliquary universalization just shipped — code, spec, and handbook tracks now all tell one story that conjure/bind/graft all require reliquary. This was the explicit prerequisite for the next pace.

## Next pace: ₢BBAA3 `cloud-build-reliquary-preflight` [rough]

**Goal**: Defense-in-depth — every Cloud Build job validates reliquary GAR-presence **from the worker pool's vantage** before expensive work runs. Host-side preflight only proves the Director's host can reach GAR; it doesn't catch airgap-VPC routing divergence, Director↔Mason IAM splits, or submit↔execute time skew.

**Surface (from docket)**:
1. New step script `rbgjr*` (or analogous) — HEAD-checks against reliquary tool images + enshrined base anchors via gcloud/alpine
2. Step-assembly prepend in 4 entry points: `rbfd_build` (conjure), `zrbfd_mirror_submit` (bind), `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit` (graft), `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` (enshrine)
3. Spec updates: RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE — declare the in-pool preflight step
4. RBS0 cloud-build-step contexts paragraph — extend to mention in-pool preflight contract

**Wrap criterion**:
- Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path CB job
- Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) → clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message
- Green case overhead acceptable (~5s)

**Cross-pace dep**: ₢BBAA4 (reliquary-universal-required) — ✅ landed.

## Name assessment
`cloud-build-reliquary-preflight` fits the docket well — describes the in-pool defense-in-depth check. Keep.

## Proposed approach
- **First**: study the four submit/build entry points to understand step-assembly shape — they each prepend to a `${RBE_BUILD_STEPS}` (or similar) block, and the prepend mechanics may differ. This is the architectural read.
- **Then**: design the step script. Open question — does it use the gcloud or alpine reliquary tool image? Alpine + curl/wget HEAD is lighter; gcloud has built-in auth but is heavier. The chicken-and-egg note specifies the step's own image is implicitly validated by CB pulling it, so the choice affects which 5 vs 6 images are covered.
- **Then**: sequential plumb across the 4 entry points + 4 specs + RBS0 paragraph.
- **Finally**: empirical wrap — green run + injected-miss (post-jettison) run, for at least one ordain mode.

**Execution strategy**: Sequential, opus-level for the step script design (structured error format, image-choice judgment, prepend-mechanics audit), then sonnet-level mechanical for the spec/handbook plumb-through and remaining entry-point integrations. Cannot parallelize the entry-point edits — they share patterns to study first.

**Status note**: Pace is tagged `[rough]`. Docket is actually well-developed — Character through Out-of-scope all present — but the rough tag suggests you may want to groom/reslate before mounting if any sharpening is needed. Otherwise, ready to proceed.

Want me to start with the step-assembly study across the four entry points, or do you want to groom/reslate first?

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Director Build - ordain, conjure, enshrine, kludge, mirror, graft operations (director credentials)

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBFD_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfd multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBFD_SOURCED=1

# Source shared Foundry Core module
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh"

# Source Foundry Verify module (ordain cross-module calls: rbfv_vouch, zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit)
source "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh"

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfd_*)

zrbfd_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFD_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfd already kindled"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle shared Foundry Core infrastructure'
  zrbfc_kindle

  buc_log_args 'Verify service account files'
  test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE not set"
  test -f "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"   || buc_die "GCB service env file not found: ${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJ files in same Tools directory as this implementation'
  # Acronym: rbgjb = Recipe Bottle Google Json Build (step scripts in rbgjb/ dir)
  local z_self_dir="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjb"
  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJB steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJB_STEPS_DIR}"

  # RBGJV and RBGJA step dirs now owned by rbfc_FoundryCore.sh (shared assembly helpers)

  buc_log_args 'RBGJM mirror step scripts (same Tools directory)'
  # Acronym: rbgjm = Recipe Bottle Google Json Mirror (step scripts in rbgjm/ dir)
  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgjm"
  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJM steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJM_STEPS_DIR}"

  buc_log_args 'RBGJE enshrine step scripts (same Tools directory)'
  # Acronym: rbgje = Recipe Bottle Google Json Enshrine (step scripts in rbgje/ dir)
  readonly ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR="${z_self_dir}/rbgje"
  test -d "${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}"   || buc_die "RBGJE steps directory not found: ${ZRBFD_RBGJE_STEPS_DIR}"

  # RBGJI inscribe step scripts now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh

  # Delete, token, inscribe, reliquary prefixes now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh
  # Vouch and about prefixes now owned by rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh

  buc_log_args 'Define stitch operation file prefix (postfixed per step id)'
  readonly ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_stitch_"

  # Inscribe and reliquary prefixes now owned by rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh

  buc_log_args 'Define mirror operation files'
  readonly ZRBFD_MIRROR_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_mirror_"

  buc_log_args 'Define graft operation files'
  readonly ZRBFD_GRAFT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_graft_"

  buc_log_args 'Define enshrine operation files'
  readonly ZRBFD_ENSHRINE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_enshrine_"

  buc_log_args 'Define enshrine preflight files'
  readonly ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_preflight_"

  buc_log_args 'Define context push operation files'
  readonly ZRBFD_CONTEXT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfd_context_"

  buc_log_args 'Kindle verify module (cross-module calls from ordain)'
  zrbfv_kindle

  readonly ZRBFD_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfd_sentinel() {
  zrbfc_sentinel
  test "${ZRBFD_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfd not kindled - call zrbfd_kindle first"
}


# Verify reliquary tool images exist in GAR.
# Full-subtree presence check: every canonical tool image must be present at
# the stamp. Inscribe creates all 6 atomically; piecemeal jettison or registry
# damage compromises the stamp's integrity. Misses are accumulated across the
# whole tool set so the operator sees full damage in one report.
# Args: token vessel_dir
zrbfd_preflight_reliquary() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  local -r z_token="${1:-}"

110	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
111	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
112	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: token required"
113	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_preflight_reliquary: vessel_dir required"
114	
115	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
116	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY required on every ordain-path vessel — yoke a reliquary stamp via tt/${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}.sh before ordaining"
117	
118	  buc_step "Verifying reliquary tool images exist in GAR"
119	
120	  local -r z_canonical_tools=(
121	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}"
122	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}"
123	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}"
124	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}"
125	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}"
126	    "${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}"
127	  )
128	
129	  local z_missing=()
130	  local z_tool=""
131	  local z_pkg=""
132	  local z_status_file=""
133	  local z_response_file=""
134	  local z_stderr_file=""
135	  local z_http_code=""
136	
137	  for z_tool in "${z_canonical_tools[@]}"; do
138	    z_pkg="${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
139	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_status.txt"
140	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_response.txt"
141	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}reliquary_${z_tool}_stderr.txt"
142	
143	    curl --head -sS \
144	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
145	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
146	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
147	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
148	      -w "%{http_code}" \
149	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
150	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg}/manifests/${z_reliquary}" \
151	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
152	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
153	
154	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
155	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for reliquary check: ${z_tool}"
156	
157	    case "${z_http_code}" in
158	      200) buc_log_args "Reliquary tool present: ${z_tool}" ;;
159	      404) z_missing+=("${z_tool}") ;;
160	      *)   buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking reliquary tool: ${z_pkg}:${z_reliquary}" ;;
161	    esac
162	  done
163	
164	  if test "${#z_missing[@]}" -eq 0; then
165	    buc_info "Reliquary verified: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_canonical_tools[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools present)"
166	    return 0
167	  fi
168	
169	  buc_warn "Reliquary integrity check failed: ${z_reliquary} (${#z_missing[@]}/${#z_canonical_tools[@]} tools missing)"
170	  buc_bare "  The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images (gcloud, docker,"
171	  buc_bare "  syft, alpine, binfmt, skopeo) inscribed from upstream into your private GAR."
172	  buc_bare "  Air-gapped worker pools cannot pull from the public internet — the reliquary"
173	  buc_bare "  stages these tools so builds can run without egress. Piecemeal jettison is"
174	  buc_bare "  allowed but unrecoverable surgically: re-inscribe the whole stamp."
175	  buc_bare ""
176	  for z_tool in "${z_missing[@]}"; do
177	    buc_bare "  PRECHECK: GAR image not found at ${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}/${z_tool}"
178	    buc_bare "    Required by ${RBRV_SIGIL}'s RBRV_RELIQUARY=${z_reliquary}."
179	  done
180	  buc_bare ""
181	  buc_bare "  Recover by re-inscribing the reliquary, then re-yoking and re-ordaining:"
182	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY}"
183	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_YOKE_RELIQUARY}" "<new-stamp>" "${z_vessel_dir}"
184	  buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
185	  buc_die "Registry preflight failed — ${#z_missing[@]} of ${#z_canonical_tools[@]} reliquary tool images missing from GAR"
186	}
187	
188	
189	# Check concurrent build quota against regime requirements
190	# Args: token mode
191	#   mode: "gate" (die if insufficient) or "advisory" (warn if insufficient)
192	zrbfd_quota_preflight() {
193	  zrbfd_sentinel
194	
195	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
196	
197	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "zrbfd_quota_preflight: token required"
198	
199	  # Extract vCPU count from machine type (last segment after final hyphen)
200	  local -r z_vcpus="${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE##*-}"
201	  case "${z_vcpus}" in
202	    ""|0|*[!0-9]*)
203	      buc_warn "Cannot parse vCPU count from RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE='${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE}' -- skipping quota preflight"
204	      return 0
205	      ;;
206	  esac
207	
208	  buc_log_args "Machine type ${RBRR_GCB_MACHINE_TYPE} = ${z_vcpus} vCPUs"
209	
210	  # Query Service Usage consumer quota API for concurrent_private_pool_build_cpus
211	  local -r z_metric_encoded="cloudbuild.googleapis.com%2Fconcurrent_private_pool_build_cpus"
212	  local -r z_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_SERVICEUSAGE}${RBGC_SERVICEUSAGE_V1BETA1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/services/cloudbuild.googleapis.com/consumerQuotaMetrics/${z_metric_encoded}"
213	
214	  buc_step "Checking concurrent build quota"
215	  rbgu_http_json "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}" "quota_preflight"
216	
217	  local z_code=""
218	  z_code=$(rbgu_http_code_capture "quota_preflight") || z_code=""
219	  if test "${z_code}" != "200"; then
220	    buc_warn "Could not query build quota (HTTP ${z_code}) -- skipping preflight check"
221	    return 0
222	  fi
223	
224	  # Filter quota response to region-specific bucket via intermediate file
225	  rbgu_jq_file_to_file_ok "quota_preflight" "quota_region" \
226	    "[.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[] | select(.dimensions.region == \"${RBRR_GCP_REGION}\")] | .[0] // {}" \
227	    || true
228	
229	  # Extract effective limit from region bucket, then fallback to first bucket
230	  local z_limit=""
231	  z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_region" '.effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
232	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
233	    z_limit=$(rbgu_json_field_capture "quota_preflight" \
234	      '.consumerQuotaLimits[0].quotaBuckets[0].effectiveLimit') || z_limit=""
235	  fi
236	
237	  if test -z "${z_limit}"; then
238	    buc_warn "Could not extract quota limit -- skipping preflight check"
239	    return 0
240	  fi
241	
242	  # -1 means unlimited
243	  if test "${z_limit}" = "-1"; then
244	    buc_info "Quota: unlimited concurrent private pool build CPUs"
245	    return 0
246	  fi
247	
248	  # Compute max concurrent builds
249	  local -r z_max_concurrent=$((z_limit / z_vcpus))
250	
251	  buc_log_args "Quota ${z_limit} vCPUs, machine ${z_vcpus} vCPUs, max concurrent ${z_max_concurrent}, required ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"
252	
253	  if test "${z_max_concurrent}" -lt "${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS}"; then
254	    buc_warn "Build quota insufficient: ${z_limit} vCPU quota / ${z_vcpus} vCPUs per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
255	    buc_warn "Fresh depots start with a low quota. After some build activity, the Edit Quotas option becomes available."
256	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_QUOTA_BUILD}"
257	  else
258	    buc_info "Quota OK: ${z_limit} vCPU / ${z_vcpus} per build = ${z_max_concurrent} concurrent (need ${RBRR_GCB_MIN_CONCURRENT_BUILDS})"
259	  fi
260	}
261	
262	# Internal: Verify that all required images exist in GAR before submitting
263	# to Cloud Build. Checks two layers in dependency order:
264	#
265	#   1. Reliquary — co-versioned builder tool images (gcloud, docker, syft, etc.)
266	#      created by inscribe. One reliquary per depot setup. Enshrine depends on it.
267	#   2. Enshrine — upstream base images copied into private GAR, pinned by content
268	#      hash. One enshrine per base image anchor. Multiple vessels sharing the same
269	#      anchor only need one enshrine.
270	#
271	# A missing image at either layer causes a Cloud Build failure minutes later.
272	# This preflight catches it immediately with copy-paste remediation commands.
273	#
274	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY, RBRV_IMAGE_*_ANCHOR)
275	# and authentication (needs token for registry API).
276	zrbfd_registry_preflight() {
277	  zrbfd_sentinel
278	
279	  local -r z_token="${1:-}"
280	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${2:-}"
281	  test -n "${z_token}"      || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: token required"
282	  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "zrbfd_registry_preflight: vessel_dir required"
283	
284	  # --- Layer 1: Reliquary tool images ---
285	  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
286	
287	  # --- Layer 2: Enshrined base images ---
288	  # Each vessel declares base images via RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN (upstream tag) and
289	  # RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR (locator: package-path:tag). Enshrine copies the upstream
290	  # image into the enshrine namespace, pinned by content hash. The conjure
291	  # Dockerfile's FROM references the locator-resolved GAR ref, not upstream tag.
292	
293	  buc_step "Verifying enshrined base images exist in GAR"
294	
295	  local z_n=""
296	  local z_anchor_var=""
297	  local z_anchor=""
298	  local z_pkg_path=""
299	  local z_tag=""
300	  local z_origin_var=""
301	  local z_origin=""
302	  local z_any_checked="false"
303	  local z_status_file=""
304	  local z_response_file=""
305	  local z_stderr_file=""
306	  local z_http_code=""
307	
308	  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
309	    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
310	    z_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ANCHOR"
311	    z_origin="${!z_origin_var:-}"
312	    z_anchor="${!z_anchor_var:-}"
313	
314	    # Skip slots without an origin or without an anchor (pass-through images don't need enshrine)
315	    test -n "${z_origin}" || continue
316	    test -n "${z_anchor}" || continue
317	
318	    case "${z_anchor}" in
319	      *:*) : ;;
320	      *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_anchor}" ;;
321	    esac
322	    z_pkg_path="${z_anchor%:*}"
323	    z_tag="${z_anchor##*:}"
324	    test -n "${z_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
325	    test -n "${z_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_anchor_var}: ${z_anchor}"
326	
327	    z_any_checked="true"
328	    z_status_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_status.txt"
329	    z_response_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_response.txt"
330	    z_stderr_file="${ZRBFD_PREFLIGHT_PREFIX}enshrine_${z_n}_stderr.txt"
331	
332	    curl --head -sS \
333	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
334	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
335	      -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
336	      -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
337	      -w "%{http_code}" \
338	      -o "${z_response_file}" \
339	      "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_pkg_path}/manifests/${z_tag}" \
340	      > "${z_status_file}" 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
341	      || buc_die "HEAD request failed for enshrined image: ${z_anchor} — see ${z_stderr_file}"
342	
343	    z_http_code=$(<"${z_status_file}")
344	    test -n "${z_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for enshrine check"
345	
346	    if test "${z_http_code}" = "404"; then
347	      buc_warn "Enshrined base image not found: ${z_anchor} (from ${z_origin})"
348	      buc_bare "  Enshrine copies upstream base images (e.g., busybox:latest from Docker Hub) into"
349	      buc_bare "  your private GAR, pinned by content hash. Like the reliquary, this ensures"
350	      buc_bare "  air-gapped builds never reach the public internet. The anchor locator is stable"
351	      buc_bare "  until you deliberately re-enshrine to pick up a newer upstream version."
352	      buc_bare "  Multiple vessels sharing the same base image anchor need only one enshrine."
353	      buc_bare "  Run enshrine, then re-run ordain:"
354	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ENSHRINE_VESSEL}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
355	      buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_ORDAIN_HALLMARK}" "${z_vessel_dir}"
356	      buc_die "Registry preflight failed — enshrined base image missing from GAR"
357	    elif test "${z_http_code}" != "200"; then
358	      buc_die "Unexpected HTTP ${z_http_code} when checking enshrined image: ${z_anchor}"
359	    fi
360	
361	    buc_log_args "Enshrined image verified: ${z_anchor}"
362	  done
363	
364	  if test "${z_any_checked}" = "true"; then
365	    buc_info "All enshrined base images verified in GAR"
366	  fi
367	}
