rbfv_vouch() {
  zrbfv_sentinel

  local -r z_vessel_dir="${1:-}"
  local -r z_hallmark="${2:-}"

  buc_doc_brief "Vouch for an ark by mode-aware verification in Cloud Build"
  buc_doc_param "vessel_dir" "Path to vessel directory containing rbrv.env"
  buc_doc_param "hallmark" "Full hallmark (e.g., c260305133650-r260305160530)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  if test -z "${z_vessel_dir}"; then
    local z_sigils
    z_sigils=$(rbrv_list_capture) || buc_die "No vessels found"
    buc_step "Available vessels:"
    local z_sigil=""
    for z_sigil in ${z_sigils}; do
      buc_bare "        ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${z_sigil}"
    done
    buc_die "Vessel directory required"
  fi

  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Hallmark parameter required"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (vouch steps use tool images)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Gate: require about exists (about must complete before vouch)
  buc_step "Gating on about artifact existence"
  local -r z_hallmark_subtree="${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${z_hallmark}"
  local -r z_about_gate_status="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}about_status.txt"
  local -r z_about_gate_response="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}about_response.json"
  local -r z_about_gate_stderr="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}about_stderr.txt"

  curl --head -s \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
    -w "%{http_code}" \
    -o "${z_about_gate_response}" \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_hallmark_subtree}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}/manifests/${z_hallmark}" \
    > "${z_about_gate_status}" 2>"${z_about_gate_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "HEAD request failed for about artifact — see ${z_about_gate_stderr}"

  local -r z_about_http_code=$(<"${z_about_gate_status}")
  test -n "${z_about_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for about"
  test "${z_about_http_code}" = "200" \
    || buc_die "About artifact not found (HTTP ${z_about_http_code}) — about must complete before vouch"

  buc_info "About artifact confirmed: ${z_hallmark_subtree}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}:${z_hallmark}"

  # Gate: warn if vouch already exists (re-vouch)
  local -r z_vouch_gate_status="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}vouch_status.txt"
  local -r z_vouch_gate_response="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}vouch_response.json"
  local -r z_vouch_gate_stderr="${ZRBFV_VOUCH_PREFIX}vouch_stderr.txt"

  curl --head -s \
    --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}" \
    --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${z_token}" \
    -H "Accept: ${ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES}" \
    -w "%{http_code}" \
    -o "${z_vouch_gate_response}" \
    "${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${z_hallmark_subtree}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}/manifests/${z_hallmark}" \
    > "${z_vouch_gate_status}" 2>"${z_vouch_gate_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "HEAD request failed for vouch artifact — see ${z_vouch_gate_stderr}"

  local -r z_vouch_http_code=$(<"${z_vouch_gate_status}")
  test -n "${z_vouch_http_code}" || buc_die "HTTP status code is empty for vouch"
  if test "${z_vouch_http_code}" = "200"; then
    buc_warn "Re-vouch in progress: ${z_hallmark_subtree}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark} already exists"
  fi

  # All modes use Cloud Build for vouch (mode-aware verification inside the build)
  zrbfv_vouch_submit "${z_hallmark}" "${z_token}"

  buc_success "Vouch complete: ${z_hallmark}"
  buc_info "Vouch artifact: ${z_hallmark_subtree}/${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH}:${z_hallmark}"
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY,
6	#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
7	#
8	# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
9	# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON locator results to /builder/outputs/output.
10	# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
11	#
12	# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
13	# Locator shape (written back to rbrv.env): <ENSHRINES_CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
14	# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
15	# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.
16	
17	set -euo pipefail
18	echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="
19	
20	# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
21	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
22	TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
23	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
24	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
25	
26	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
27	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
28	
29	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
30	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
31	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
32	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
34	
35	# Initialize result JSON
36	RESULT='{'
37	FIRST=true
38	
39	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
40	  case "${SLOT}" in
41	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
42	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
43	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
44	  esac
45	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
46	
47	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"
48	
49	  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
50	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
51	  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
52	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
53	
54	  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
55	  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
56	  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
57	  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
58	
59	  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
60	  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
61	  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
62	  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"
63	
64	  # Compose locator (package-path:tag, no cloud prefix — caller prepends at use-site)
65	  LOCATOR="${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
66	
67	  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
68	  echo "Locator: ${LOCATOR}"
69	  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"
70	
71	  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
72	  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
73	  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"
74	
75	  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
76	  skopeo copy --all \
77	    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
78	    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
79	    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
80	    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
81	
82	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"
83	
84	  # Accumulate JSON result (no jq dependency — printf safe for sanitized values)
85	  # The "anchor" key carries the full locator (writeback target for RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR).
86	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
87	    FIRST=false
88	  else
89	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
90	  fi
91	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${LOCATOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
92	done
93	
94	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
95	
96	echo "=== Writing anchor results ==="
97	echo "${RESULT}"
98	
99	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel
100	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
101	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
102	
103	echo "=== Enshrine step complete ==="
104

# RBGJE Step 01: Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGE_GAR_HOST, _RBGE_GAR_PATH, _RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT,
#                _RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY,
#                _RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN
#
# For each non-empty ORIGIN slot: inspect upstream manifest, compute anchor,
# skopeo copy --all to GAR. Write JSON locator results to /builder/outputs/output.
# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
#
# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<ENSHRINES_ROOT>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
# Locator shape (written back to rbrv.env): <ENSHRINES_CATEGORY>/<ANCHOR>:<ANCHOR>
# Anchor as both path segment and tag — each anchor is a peer package under
# the enshrines namespace, not a tag on a shared package.

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Enshrine base images to GAR ==="

# Obtain OAuth2 token from metadata server (Mason SA)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGE_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"

# Initialize result JSON
RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}" ;;
    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}" ;;
    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue

  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} ---"

  # Inspect upstream for raw manifest (manifest list or single manifest)
  RAW_FILE="/workspace/enshrine_raw_${SLOT}.json"
  skopeo inspect --raw "docker://${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to inspect upstream: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Compute sha256 of the raw manifest
  # Note: sha256sum (coreutils), not openssl — this runs inside the skopeo reliquary container
  SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
  test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Construct anchor: sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars
  SANITIZED=$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')
  SHORT="${SHA:0:10}"
  ANCHOR="${SANITIZED}-${SHORT}"

  # Compose locator (package-path:tag, no cloud prefix — caller prepends at use-site)
  LOCATOR="${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_CATEGORY}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"

  echo "Anchor: ${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Locator: ${LOCATOR}"
  echo "Digest: sha256:${SHA}"

  # Construct GAR destination — anchor is both the package name and the tag
  DEST_REF="${_RBGE_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGE_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGE_ENSHRINES_ROOT}/${ANCHOR}:${ANCHOR}"
  echo "Dest: ${DEST_REF}"

  # Copy upstream to GAR with anchor tag, preserving manifest list
  skopeo copy --all \
    "docker://${ORIGIN}" \
    "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
    --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: skopeo copy failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "Slot ${SLOT} enshrined: ${ANCHOR}"

  # Accumulate JSON result (no jq dependency — printf safe for sanitized values)
  # The "anchor" key carries the full locator (writeback target for RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR).
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"anchor\":\"${LOCATOR}\",\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\",\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\"}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing anchor results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

# Write to buildStepOutputs channel
mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Enshrine step complete ==="

lastPrompt: start the study.

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJM Step 01: Mirror image from upstream to GAR via skopeo
# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGA_GAR_HOST, _RBGA_GAR_PATH, _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT,
#                _RBGA_HALLMARK, _RBGA_BIND_SOURCE
#
# Registry-to-registry copy preserving multi-platform manifest lists.
# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
# Public upstream images need no auth.
#
# Destination uses canonical AAK shape:
#   ${gar_host}/${gar_path}/${HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${hallmark}/image:${hallmark}
# matching rbgja02 (syft), rbgja04 (about), rbgjv03 (vouch).

set -euo pipefail

test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_HOST missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_PATH missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"  || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"        || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARK missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"     || { echo "_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE missing"     >&2; exit 1; }

DEST_REF="${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${_RBGA_HALLMARK}/image:${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"

echo "=== Mirroring bind image via skopeo ==="
echo "Source: ${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"
echo "Dest:   ${DEST_REF}"

# Obtain OAuth2 token from Cloud Build metadata server (Mason SA)
echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

# Extract access_token (no jq dependency — use grep+cut)
TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

# Registry-to-registry copy preserving multi-platform manifest
skopeo copy --all \
  "docker://${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}" \
  "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
  --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
  || { echo "skopeo copy failed" >&2; exit 1; }

echo "Image mirrored: ${DEST_REF}"
echo "=== Mirror step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJI Step 01: Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (always pullable — Google-hosted)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGN_GAR_HOST, _RBGN_GAR_PATH,
5	#                _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGN_RELIQUARY
6	#
7	# Mirrors all 6 GCB step/tool images from upstream registries to a datestamped
8	# GAR namespace (reliquary). Co-versioned: all images in one pass, one datestamp.
9	# Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB steps on amd64 workers.
10	# Outputs JSON manifest with tool→digest mapping via /builder/outputs/output.
11	#
12	# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>:<RELIQUARY>
13	# Tag = the reliquary datestamp itself (immutable-datestamp-as-tag).
14	
15	set -euo pipefail
16	echo "=== Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary ==="
17	echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
18	
19	# Tool image manifest: short-name|upstream-ref
20	# This is the authoritative list of tool images for GCB step execution.
21	# gcloud and docker are Google-hosted (gcr.io); rest are third-party.
22	MANIFEST=(
23	  "gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"
24	  "docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest"
25	  "alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
26	  "syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest"
27	  "binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest"
28	  "skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest"
29	)
30	
31	RESULT='{'
32	FIRST=true
33	
34	for ENTRY in "${MANIFEST[@]}"; do
35	  NAME="${ENTRY%%|*}"
36	  UPSTREAM="${ENTRY#*|}"
37	
38	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} ---"
39	
40	  DEST="${_RBGN_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGN_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}/${NAME}:${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
41	  echo "Dest: ${DEST}"
42	
43	  docker pull "${UPSTREAM}" \
44	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to pull ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
45	
46	  # Extract pulled digest from RepoDigests (format: repo@sha256:...)
47	  DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "${UPSTREAM}" 2>/dev/null) || DIGEST=""
48	  DIGEST="${DIGEST##*@}"
49	
50	  docker tag "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
51	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to tag ${NAME}" >&2; exit 1; }
52	
53	  docker push "${DEST}" \
54	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to push ${NAME} to GAR" >&2; exit 1; }
55	
56	  echo "${NAME} inscribed: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
57	
58	  # Accumulate JSON result
59	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
60	    FIRST=false
61	  else
62	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
63	  fi
64	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"${NAME}\":{\"upstream\":\"${UPSTREAM}\",\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\"}"
65	done
66	
67	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
68	
69	echo "=== Writing inscribe results ==="
70	echo "${RESULT}"
71	
72	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
73	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
74	
75	echo "=== Inscribe step complete ==="

#!/bin/bash
# RBGJI Step 01: Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary
# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (always pullable — Google-hosted)
# Substitutions: _RBGN_GAR_HOST, _RBGN_GAR_PATH,
#                _RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGN_RELIQUARY
#
# Mirrors all 6 GCB step/tool images from upstream registries to a datestamped
# GAR namespace (reliquary). Co-versioned: all images in one pass, one datestamp.
# Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB steps on amd64 workers.
# Outputs JSON manifest with tool→digest mapping via /builder/outputs/output.
#
# Image URI shape: <host>/<path>/<RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>:<RELIQUARY>
# Tag = the reliquary datestamp itself (immutable-datestamp-as-tag).

set -euo pipefail
echo "=== Inscribe tool images to GAR reliquary ==="
echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"

# Tool image manifest: short-name|upstream-ref
# This is the authoritative list of tool images for GCB step execution.
# gcloud and docker are Google-hosted (gcr.io); rest are third-party.
MANIFEST=(
  "gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"
  "docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest"
  "alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
  "syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest"
  "binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest"
  "skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest"
)

RESULT='{'
FIRST=true

for ENTRY in "${MANIFEST[@]}"; do
  NAME="${ENTRY%%|*}"
  UPSTREAM="${ENTRY#*|}"

  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} ---"

  DEST="${_RBGN_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGN_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}/${NAME}:${_RBGN_RELIQUARY}"
  echo "Dest: ${DEST}"

  docker pull "${UPSTREAM}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to pull ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }

  # Extract pulled digest from RepoDigests (format: repo@sha256:...)
  DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "${UPSTREAM}" 2>/dev/null) || DIGEST=""
  DIGEST="${DIGEST##*@}"

  docker tag "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to tag ${NAME}" >&2; exit 1; }

  docker push "${DEST}" \
    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to push ${NAME} to GAR" >&2; exit 1; }

  echo "${NAME} inscribed: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"

  # Accumulate JSON result
  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
    FIRST=false
  else
    RESULT="${RESULT},"
  fi
  RESULT="${RESULT}\"${NAME}\":{\"upstream\":\"${UPSTREAM}\",\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\"}"
done

RESULT="${RESULT}}"

echo "=== Writing inscribe results ==="
echo "${RESULT}"

mkdir -p /builder/outputs
printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output

echo "=== Inscribe step complete ==="

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJAM Step 04: Build and push multi-platform metadata (about) container
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker
4	# Substitutions: _RBGA_GAR_HOST, _RBGA_GAR_PATH, _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGA_HALLMARK, _RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT
6	#
7	# Builds a FROM scratch container where TARGETARCH/TARGETVARIANT
8	# auto-args select the per-platform SBOM and build_info files.
9	# For single-platform images, builds a single-platform container.
10	# For multi-platform images, uses buildx --push.
11	#
12	# File naming convention (matches step 02/03 outputs):
13	#   sbom-{arch}{variant}.json       -> /sbom.json
14	#   build_info-{arch}{variant}.json -> /build_info.json
15	#   recipe.txt                      -> /recipe.txt (optional, from step 03)
16	#
17	# No QEMU needed: scratch images have no executables — just file copies
18	# with platform annotations.
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}"          || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_HOST missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
23	test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}"          || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_PATH missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
24	test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"    || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT missing"    >&2; exit 1; }
25	test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"          || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARK missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
26	test -n "${_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}" || { echo "_RBGA_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT missing" >&2; exit 1; }
27	
28	test -s platforms.txt         || { echo "platforms.txt not found (step 01)" >&2; exit 1; }
29	test -s platform_suffixes.txt || { echo "platform_suffixes.txt not found (step 01)" >&2; exit 1; }
30	test -s platform_count.txt    || { echo "platform_count.txt not found (step 01)" >&2; exit 1; }

100	  z_rbfc_tool_syft=""
101	  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt=""
102	  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo=""
103	
104	  readonly ZRBFC_KINDLED=1
105	}
106	
107	zrbfc_sentinel() {
108	  test "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfc not kindled - call zrbfc_kindle first"
109	}
110	
111	# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
112	# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
113	# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
114	# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
115	zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
116	  zrbfc_sentinel
117	
118	  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
119	  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
120	    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"
121	
122	  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
123	  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
124	  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"

z_rbfc_tool_syft=""
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt=""
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo=""

  readonly ZRBFC_KINDLED=1
}

zrbfc_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbfc not kindled - call zrbfc_kindle first"
}

# Internal: Resolve tool image references from reliquary.
# Must be called after vessel load (reads RBRV_RELIQUARY).
# Sets module-level z_rbfc_tool_* mutable kindle state for downstream step assembly.
# Idempotent — safe to call multiple times per invocation.
zrbfc_resolve_tool_images() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_reliquary="${RBRV_RELIQUARY:-}"
  test -n "${z_reliquary}" \
    || buc_die "RBRV_RELIQUARY is required — run inscribe to create a reliquary first"

  local -r z_rqy_prefix="${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}/${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_gcloud="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_GCLOUD}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_docker="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_DOCKER}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_alpine="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_ALPINE}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_syft="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SYFT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_binfmt="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_BINFMT}:${z_reliquary}"
  z_rbfc_tool_skopeo="${z_rqy_prefix}/${RBGC_RELIQUARY_TOOL_SKOPEO}:${z_reliquary}"
  buc_log_args "Tool images resolved from reliquary: ${z_reliquary}"
}

# Validate that a vessel sigil is non-empty and corresponds to a known vessel.
# On missing or invalid sigil, lists available vessels and dies.
# Does NOT resolve to a directory — use zrbfc_resolve_vessel for that.

795	This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
796	All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
797	The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
798	Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
799	+
800	In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
801	+
802	Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
803	The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
804	+
805	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
806	The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
807	
808	[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
809	//axl_voices axk_premise
810	{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
811	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
812	The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
813	Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
814	Batched invest is not supported — by design.
815	+
816	Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.
817	The operator-driven move step is the deliberate promotion gate from producer output to role-production consumer; it also acts as the operator's explicit acknowledgement of which identity owns the role at runtime, since `RBRA` files encode exactly one identity at a time.
818	Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion.

file.content:
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.
+
Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.
The operator-driven move step is the deliberate promotion gate from producer output to role-production consumer; it also acts as the operator's explicit acknowledgement of which identity owns the role at runtime, since `RBRA` files encode exactly one identity at a time.
Move (not copy) prevents the assay slot from accumulating stale credential bytes after promotion.

content.subject: Map four ordain-path entry points to step-assembly
content.description: Conjure (zrbfd_stitch_build_json line 642): combines extract-context + image-steps + about-steps via jq -s '.[0]+.[1]' chain. Bind (zrbfd_mirror_submit line 1514): combines mirror-step + about-steps. Enshrine (zrbfd_enshrine_submit line 905): single skopeo step, no chain. Graft (zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit) — needs inspection. All four use 'jq -s [.[0]+.[1]]' as the combine pattern; preflight prepends naturally as a leftmost slurpfile arg. Token + auth already happen in rbfd_build/rbfd_mirror/rbfd_graft outer functions before submit; enshrine's caller (rbfd_enshrine) similarly.

content.subject: Inventory existing rbgj* step scripts for conventions
content.description: Read existing step scripts in Tools/rbk/rbgj{a,b,e,i,m,v}/ to identify shared conventions: shebang, error handling, env-var contracts, output shape. The new rbgjr* step should match these patterns.

content.subject: Resolve tool-image choice for the preflight step
content.description: Decide between gcloud and alpine reliquary tool images for HEAD-check execution. Tradeoffs: gcloud has built-in auth + heavy; alpine + curl/wget is light but needs explicit auth. Chicken-and-egg: the step's own image is implicitly validated by CB pulling it, so the choice affects which 5 vs 6 images are covered by the explicit HEAD-check.

content.subject: Determine HEAD-check target set per ordain mode
content.description: Each ordain mode has a different set of images CB will pull. Conjure pulls all reliquary tools + (if enshrine slot present) base anchors. Bind pulls skopeo + reliquary. Graft pulls about/vouch reliquary tools. Enshrine pulls gcloud + skopeo. Document the per-mode target set so the preflight checks the right images for its caller.

content.content: Updated task #2 status

input.description: Conclusion: alpine. Three CB step scripts (rbgja02 syft, rbgje enshrine, rbgjm mirror) already use the metadata-server token fetch pattern; alpine has curl + grep + cut, sufficient for HEAD-checks against GAR. Alpine being the validating image means the preflight HEAD-checks the OTHER 5 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo) — alpine itself is implicitly validated by Cloud Build's pull. gcloud is overkill (~500MB, slow pull, not needed for raw HTTP HEAD).

content.content: Updated task #3 description, status

input.description: Mirror host-side semantics one-for-one: HEAD-check ALL 6 reliquary tools (the host preflight does this regardless of mode), plus enshrined base anchors per RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR slots (host preflight already iterates all 3 slots, empty slots continue). Per-mode subset checks would be over-clever; uniform check matches host-side semantics and the structured failure message ('this pool can't see «TOOL»') stays mode-agnostic. Note: if alpine is chosen as the validating image, the preflight skips alpine itself — covers 5 of 6 tools.

content.content: Updated task #4 description, status

content.text:
## Study results

### 1. The four entry points map cleanly — same combine pattern across all

All four submit functions use the same `jq -s '.[0] + .[1]'` combine idiom to splice step arrays. Prepending an in-pool preflight as step 0 fits naturally everywhere — extend the slurp chain by one file.

| Entry point | File:line | Current step shape | Prepend mechanics |
|---|---|---|---|
| Conjure (`rbfd_build` via stitch) | `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:642` | `extract-context` + image-steps + about-steps | Already prepends `extract-context` via `jq -s` — preflight inserts before |
| Bind (`zrbfd_mirror_submit`) | `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:1514` | mirror-skopeo + about-steps | Add preflight as third slurp in the combine |
| Graft (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`) | `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh:309` | about-steps + vouch-steps | Add preflight as third slurp |
| Enshrine (`zrbfd_enshrine_submit`) | `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh:905` | enshrine-skopeo only (1 step) | Build 2-step array directly |

### 2. Important docket gap: vouch CB job not in surface

There are **seven** CB submission points in the codebase, not four. The docket lists only four entry points, but the wrap criterion says "every ordain-path Cloud Build job."

| CB submission | Caller | In docket surface? |
|---|---|---|
| Conjure image+about | `rbfd_build` → stitch | ✓ |
| Bind mirror+about | `rbfd_mirror` → `zrbfd_mirror_submit` | ✓ |
| Graft about+vouch | `rbfd_ordain` → `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit` | ✓ |
| Enshrine | `rbfd_enshrine` → `zrbfd_enshrine_submit` | ✓ |
| **Vouch (separate)** | `rbfv_vouch` → `zrbfv_vouch_submit` (`rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh:670`) | ✗ |
| Standalone about | `rbfv_about` → `zrbfv_about_submit` | ✗ (operator-recovery, arguably not ordain) |
| Inscribe (reliquary creation) | `rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh:148` | N/A — chicken-and-egg |

**Vouch is in every ordain flow** for conjure and bind: `rbfd_ordain` calls `rbfd_build`/`rbfd_mirror` (which submits image+about CB), then calls `rbfv_vouch` (which submits a separate vouch CB). For graft, vouch is folded into the combined about+vouch CB job. So conjure and bind each have **two** ordain-path CB jobs, only the first of which is in the docket's surface list.

Also, `rbfv_vouch` does **not** call `zrbfd_registry_preflight` either — there's a host-side preflight gap too, parallel to the in-pool one. BBAA4 just plugged this for graft/bind/conjure ordain entry points but missed vouch.

### 3. Step-script pattern is established

All existing step scripts share strong conventions: `set -euo pipefail`, header comment block listing builder + substitutions + contract, env-var checks, `|| { echo "FATAL:..." >&2; exit 1; }` guards, metadata-server token fetch (`http://metadata.google.internal/...`) for GAR auth without jq dependency. Three scripts (`rbgja02`, `rbgje01`, `rbgjm01`) already do exactly the kind of HEAD/inspect work the preflight needs.

### 4. Recommended choices

- **Image**: alpine reliquary tool. Has curl + grep + cut. Light, established as a CB step image elsewhere. Alpine being the validating image means the preflight covers the **other 5** reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo) — alpine itself is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Document this boundary in spec per the docket's chicken-and-egg note.
- **Coverage per mode**: Mirror the host-side `zrbfd_preflight_reliquary` semantics — always all 6 (or 5 if alpine self-excluded) tools regardless of mode, plus enshrined anchors via the existing slot iteration. Per-mode subset checks would be over-clever; structured error stays mode-agnostic.
- **Step prefix**: `rbgjr*` (R for **r**eliquary preflight, matching the docket's "rbgjr* or analogous") with substitutions namespace `_RBGR_*`.

### 5. The decision before mounting

The docket's surface list is incomplete. Two paths:

- **(A) Reslate the docket** to add `zrbfv_vouch_submit` as a fifth prepend point and clarify scope on standalone-about. Cleaner spec, larger surface.
- **(B) Mount as written** — prepend on the four enumerated points; the standalone vouch CB stays uncovered. Smaller scope, but contradicts the wrap criterion's "every ordain-path Cloud Build job" phrasing.

I'd recommend (A) — the wrap criterion's phrasing is unambiguous, and `zrbfv_vouch_submit` follows exactly the same `jq -s` combine pattern, so the marginal cost of including it is tiny. Standalone-about is the genuinely-debatable case (it's `rbfv_about` user-driven recovery, not part of the automatic ordain chain) — easy to leave out of scope.

Want to reslate, or proceed as-is?

lastPrompt: start the study.

385	# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
386	zrbfc_assemble_about_steps() {
387	  zrbfc_sentinel
388	
389	  local -r z_output_file="$1"
390	  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"
391	
392	  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
393	  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
394	  local -r z_about_step_defs=(
395	    "rbgja01-discover-platforms.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|discover-platforms"
396	    "rbgja02-syft-per-platform.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|syft-per-platform"
397	    "rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|build-info-per-platform"
398	    "rbgja04-assemble-push-about.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-about"
399	  )
400	
401	  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize about steps JSON"
402	
403	  local z_adef=""
404	  local z_ascript=""
405	  local z_abuilder=""
406	  local z_aentrypoint=""
407	  local z_aid=""
408	  local z_ascript_path=""
409	  local z_abody_file=""
410	  local z_aescaped_file=""
411	  local z_asteps_file=""
412	  local z_abody=""
413	  local z_ashebang=""
414	
415	  for z_adef in "${z_about_step_defs[@]}"; do
416	    IFS='|' read -r z_ascript z_abuilder z_aentrypoint z_aid <<< "${z_adef}"
417	    z_ascript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}/${z_ascript}"
418	    z_abody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_body.txt"
419	    z_aescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_escaped.txt"
420	    z_asteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_steps.json"
421	
422	    test -f "${z_ascript_path}" || buc_die "About step script not found: ${z_ascript_path}"
423	
424	    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_aid} (skip shebang)"
425	    tail -n +2 "${z_ascript_path}" > "${z_abody_file}" \
426	      || buc_die "Failed to read about step script: ${z_ascript_path}"
427	    z_abody=$(<"${z_abody_file}")
428	    test -n "${z_abody}" || buc_die "Empty about script body: ${z_ascript_path}"
429

431	    z_abody="${z_abody//\$\{ZRBF_TOOL_SYFT\}/${z_rbfc_tool_syft}}"
432	
433	    case "${z_aentrypoint}" in
434	      bash)    z_ashebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
435	      sh)      z_ashebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
436	      python3) z_ashebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
437	      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_aentrypoint}" ;;
438	    esac
439	    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_ashebang}" "${z_abody}" > "${z_aescaped_file}" \

441

443	    jq \
444	      --arg name "${z_abuilder}" \
445	      --arg id "${z_aid}" \
446	      --rawfile script "${z_aescaped_file}" \
447	      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
448	      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_asteps_file}" \
449	      || buc_die "Failed to append about step ${z_aid} to JSON"
450	    mv "${z_asteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \

452	  done

# Internal: assemble about step scripts into JSON array file
# Args: output_file temp_prefix
# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
zrbfc_assemble_about_steps() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_output_file="$1"
  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"

  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
  local -r z_about_step_defs=(
    "rbgja01-discover-platforms.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|discover-platforms"
    "rbgja02-syft-per-platform.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|syft-per-platform"
    "rbgja03-build-info-per-platform.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|build-info-per-platform"
    "rbgja04-assemble-push-about.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-about"
  )

  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize about steps JSON"

  local z_adef=""
  local z_ascript=""
  local z_abuilder=""
  local z_aentrypoint=""
  local z_aid=""
  local z_ascript_path=""
  local z_abody_file=""
  local z_aescaped_file=""
  local z_asteps_file=""
  local z_abody=""
  local z_ashebang=""

  for z_adef in "${z_about_step_defs[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_ascript z_abuilder z_aentrypoint z_aid <<< "${z_adef}"
    z_ascript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJA_STEPS_DIR}/${z_ascript}"
    z_abody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_body.txt"
    z_aescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_escaped.txt"
    z_asteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_aid}_steps.json"

    test -f "${z_ascript_path}" || buc_die "About step script not found: ${z_ascript_path}"

    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_aid} (skip shebang)"
    tail -n +2 "${z_ascript_path}" > "${z_abody_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read about step script: ${z_ascript_path}"
    z_abody=$(<"${z_abody_file}")
    test -n "${z_abody}" || buc_die "Empty about script body: ${z_ascript_path}"

    buc_log_args "Baking pinned image refs into script text"
    z_abody="${z_abody//\$\{ZRBF_TOOL_SYFT\}/${z_rbfc_tool_syft}}"

    case "${z_aentrypoint}" in
      bash)    z_ashebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
      sh)      z_ashebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
      python3) z_ashebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_aentrypoint}" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_ashebang}" "${z_abody}" > "${z_aescaped_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write about script body for ${z_aid}"

    buc_log_args "Appending about step ${z_aid} to JSON array"
    jq \
      --arg name "${z_abuilder}" \
      --arg id "${z_aid}" \
      --rawfile script "${z_aescaped_file}" \
      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_asteps_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append about step ${z_aid} to JSON"
    mv "${z_asteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update about steps JSON for ${z_aid}"
  done
}

# Internal: assemble vouch step scripts into JSON array file
# Args: output_file temp_prefix
# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_* image refs from module state
zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_output_file="$1"
  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"

  # Step definitions: script|builder|entrypoint|id
  # Delimiter is | because image refs contain colons (sha256 digests)
  local -r z_vouch_step_defs=(
    "rbgjv01-download-verifier.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}|sh|prepare-keys"
    "rbgjv02-verify-provenance.py|${z_rbfc_tool_gcloud}|python3|verify-provenance"
    "rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh|${z_rbfc_tool_docker}|bash|assemble-push-vouch"
  )

  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize vouch steps JSON"

  local z_vdef=""
  local z_vscript=""
  local z_vbuilder=""
  local z_ventrypoint=""
  local z_vid=""
  local z_vscript_path=""
  local z_vbody_file=""
  local z_vescaped_file=""
  local z_vsteps_file=""
  local z_vbody=""
  local z_vshebang=""

  for z_vdef in "${z_vouch_step_defs[@]}"; do
    IFS='|' read -r z_vscript z_vbuilder z_ventrypoint z_vid <<< "${z_vdef}"
    z_vscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJV_STEPS_DIR}/${z_vscript}"
    z_vbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_body.txt"
    z_vescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_escaped.txt"
    z_vsteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}${z_vid}_steps.json"

    test -f "${z_vscript_path}" || buc_die "Vouch step script not found: ${z_vscript_path}"

    buc_log_args "Reading script body for ${z_vid} (skip shebang)"
    tail -n +2 "${z_vscript_path}" > "${z_vbody_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to read vouch step script: ${z_vscript_path}"
    z_vbody=$(<"${z_vbody_file}")
    test -n "${z_vbody}" || buc_die "Empty vouch script body: ${z_vscript_path}"

    case "${z_ventrypoint}" in
      bash)    z_vshebang="#!/bin/bash" ;;
      sh)      z_vshebang="#!/bin/sh" ;;
      python3) z_vshebang="#!/usr/bin/env python3" ;;
      *)       buc_die "Unknown entrypoint: ${z_ventrypoint}" ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n%s' "${z_vshebang}" "${z_vbody}" > "${z_vescaped_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to write vouch script body for ${z_vid}"

    buc_log_args "Appending vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON array"
    jq \
      --arg name "${z_vbuilder}" \
      --arg id "${z_vid}" \
      --rawfile script "${z_vescaped_file}" \
      '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
      "${z_output_file}" > "${z_vsteps_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to append vouch step ${z_vid} to JSON"
    mv "${z_vsteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
      || buc_die "Failed to update vouch steps JSON for ${z_vid}"
  done
}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Foundry Core - shared infrastructure for all Foundry child modules

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion guard (silent skip — rbfc is sourced by multiple child modules)
test -z "${ZRBFC_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
ZRBFC_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbfc_*)

zrbfc_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBFC_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbfc already kindled"

  # Validate environment
  zburd_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Check required GCB/GAR environment variables'
  zrbgc_sentinel

  buc_log_args 'Module Variables (ZRBFC_*)'
  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE="https://cloudbuild.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_API_BASE="https://artifactregistry.googleapis.com/v1"
  readonly ZRBFC_CLOUD_QUERY_BASE="https://console.cloud.google.com/cloud-build/builds"

  readonly ZRBFC_GCB_PROJECT_BUILDS_URL="${ZRBFC_GCB_API_BASE}/projects/${RBGD_GCB_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/builds"
  readonly ZRBFC_GAR_PACKAGE_BASE="projects/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  buc_log_args 'Trigger dispatch endpoints'
  readonly ZRBFC_TRIGGERS_URL="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CLOUDBUILD}${RBGC_CLOUDBUILD_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBGD_GCB_REGION}/triggers"

  buc_log_args 'Registry API endpoints'
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH="${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBFC_REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_HOST}/v2/${ZRBFC_REGISTRY_PATH}"

  buc_log_args 'Media types for registry operations'
  readonly ZRBFC_ACCEPT_MANIFEST_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"

  buc_log_args 'Define build polling files'
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_ID_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_id.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_STATUS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_status.json"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_START_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_start.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_BUILD_FINISH_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_build_finish.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define git info file (used by stitch)'
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_INFO_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_git_info.json"

  buc_log_args 'Define git metadata files (shared across about/mirror submissions)'
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_git_"
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_COMMIT_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}commit.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_BRANCH_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}branch.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_GIT_REPO_FILE="${ZRBFC_GIT_PREFIX}repo.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define validation files'
  readonly ZRBFC_STATUS_CHECK_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_status_check.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Vessel-related files'
  readonly ZRBFC_VESSEL_SIGIL_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_sigil.txt"
  readonly ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbfc_vessel_resolved_dir.txt"

  buc_log_args 'Define output files (BURD_OUTPUT_DIR — persists after dispatch)'

file.content:
rbfd_enshrine() {
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Enshrine upstream base images to GAR via Cloud Build"
  buc_doc_param "vessel" "Vessel sigil or path to vessel directory"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  # Resolve vessel argument (sigil or path) and load
  zrbfc_resolve_vessel "${1:-}"
  local -r z_vessel_dir=$(<"${ZRBFC_VESSEL_RESOLVED_DIR_FILE}")
  test -n "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Empty resolved vessel path"
  zrbfc_load_vessel "${z_vessel_dir}"
  test "${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-}" = "conjure" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' is not a conjure vessel (mode: ${RBRV_VESSEL_MODE:-unset})"

  # Check at least one slot declares an upstream ORIGIN. ANCHOR is a derived
  # value computed by this operation from ORIGIN; a vessel with no ORIGIN has
  # nothing for enshrine to mirror.
  local z_has_origin=false
  local z_n=""
  local z_origin_var=""
  for z_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_n}_ORIGIN"
    test -z "${!z_origin_var:-}" || z_has_origin=true
  done
  test "${z_has_origin}" = "true" \
    || buc_die "Vessel '${RBRV_SIGIL}' declares no upstream base-image slots (RBRV_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN)"

  # Resolve tool images from reliquary (enshrine uses skopeo from reliquary)
  zrbfc_resolve_tool_images

  # Authenticate as Director
  buc_step "Loading Director RBRA credentials"
  source "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Failed to source Director RBRA"

  buc_step "Authenticating as Director"
  local z_token=""
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to get Director OAuth token"

  # Verify reliquary tool images exist before submitting
  zrbfd_preflight_reliquary "${z_token}" "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Always submit the Cloud Build job. The CB step inspects current upstream
  # for each non-empty ORIGIN slot, computes the content-addressed locator
  # from the live manifest digest, and copies into GAR. Extract overwrites
  # whatever was previously committed in RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR with the just-
  # computed value — the ANCHOR is a derived artifact of the current upstream,
  # not a pin.
  zrbfd_enshrine_submit "${z_token}"

  local -r z_rbrv_file="${z_vessel_dir}/rbrv.env"
  zrbfd_enshrine_extract_anchors "${z_rbrv_file}"

  buc_success "Enshrine complete for vessel: ${RBRV_SIGIL}"
}

# Internal: Submit enshrine Cloud Build job
# Single step: skopeo inspect + copy for each ORIGIN slot, returning anchors via buildStepOutputs.
zrbfd_enshrine_submit() {

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
# RBGJR Step 01: In-pool reliquary preflight — HEAD-check tool images and enshrined anchors
# Builder: alpine (from reliquary)
# Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT,
#                _RBGR_RELIQUARY,
#                _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_1, _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_2, _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_3
#
# Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build job validates reliquary GAR-presence
# from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs. Catches divergence
# the host-side preflight cannot — airgap pool private-VPC routing, IAM split
# between Director and Mason service accounts, time skew between submission and
# execution.
#
# Coverage:
#   - Reliquary tool images (5 of 6): gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
#     Alpine (this step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build
#     pulling it for step execution; failure to pull alpine produces Cloud
#     Build's own "image not pullable" error before this script runs.
#   - Enshrined base anchor locators (slots 1/2/3 if non-empty). Conjure
#     populates these; bind, graft, and enshrine pass them through empty.
#
# Failure mode: structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each
# missed item. Operator remediation: re-inscribe the reliquary, re-yoke the
# vessel, re-ordain the hallmark.
#
# Auth: Mason SA via Cloud Build metadata server. HEAD requests via busybox
# wget (no curl in alpine by default; apk add not viable on airgap pools).
# wget exit code is the binary signal — 0 for HTTP 2xx, non-zero otherwise.

set -euo pipefail

test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_HOST missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"          || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_GAR_PATH missing"          >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}"  || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
test -n "${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"         || { echo "FATAL: _RBGR_RELIQUARY missing"         >&2; exit 1; }

echo "=== In-pool reliquary preflight ==="
echo "Reliquary: ${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"
echo "GAR base:  ${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"

echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
TOKEN_JSON=$(wget -q -O - \
  --header="Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }

TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "FATAL: Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }

ACCEPT_MTYPES="application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json,application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.list.v2+json,application/vnd.oci.image.index.v1+json,application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json"

REGISTRY_API_BASE="https://${_RBGR_GAR_HOST}/v2/${_RBGR_GAR_PATH}"

MISSES=""

# Tool list mirrors the inscribe step manifest (rbgji01) minus alpine.
# Each tool's package path: <RELIQUARIES_ROOT>/<RELIQUARY>/<NAME>; tag = <RELIQUARY>.
for TOOL in gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo; do
  PKG_PATH="${_RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}/${TOOL}"
  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${_RBGR_RELIQUARY}"

  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${_RBGR_RELIQUARY} ---"
  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
       "${URL}"; then
    echo "  PRESENT: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
  else
    echo "  MISSING: reliquary-tool ${TOOL}"
    MISSES="${MISSES} reliquary-tool:${TOOL}"
  fi
done

# Enshrine slots — empty for non-conjure modes; conjure populates from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR.
# Locator format: <package-path>:<tag>; package path is itself prefixed by the
# enshrine namespace already (e.g. rbi_es/<anchor>:<anchor>).
for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
  case "${SLOT}" in
    1) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_1:-}" ;;
    2) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_2:-}" ;;
    3) LOCATOR="${_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_3:-}" ;;
  esac
  test -n "${LOCATOR}" || continue

  case "${LOCATOR}" in
    *:*) : ;;
    *)   echo "FATAL: invalid enshrine locator format (expected package-path:tag) slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1 ;;
  esac
  PKG_PATH="${LOCATOR%:*}"
  TAG="${LOCATOR##*:}"
  test -n "${PKG_PATH}" || { echo "FATAL: empty package path in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
  test -n "${TAG}"      || { echo "FATAL: empty tag in locator slot ${SLOT}: ${LOCATOR}" >&2; exit 1; }
  URL="${REGISTRY_API_BASE}/${PKG_PATH}/manifests/${TAG}"

  echo "--- HEAD ${PKG_PATH}:${TAG} (enshrine slot ${SLOT}) ---"
  if wget -q -O /dev/null \
       --header="Authorization: Bearer ${TOKEN}" \
       --header="Accept: ${ACCEPT_MTYPES}" \
       "${URL}"; then
    echo "  PRESENT: enshrine-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
  else
    echo "  MISSING: enshrine-anchor slot ${SLOT}"
    MISSES="${MISSES} enshrine-anchor:${LOCATOR}"
  fi
done

if [ -z "${MISSES}" ]; then
  echo "=== Reliquary preflight passed — pool sees all tools and enshrined anchors ==="
  exit 0
fi

echo "" >&2
echo "=== Reliquary preflight FAILED — this pool can't see: ===" >&2
for MISS in ${MISSES}; do
  echo "  «${MISS}»" >&2
done
echo "" >&2
echo "Defense-in-depth: every Cloud Build verifies reliquary GAR-presence from" >&2
echo "the worker pool's vantage. The Director's host-side preflight passed, but" >&2
echo "the Mason pool cannot reach one or more required artifacts. Likely causes:" >&2
echo "  - airgap pool private-VPC routing missing for these artifacts" >&2
echo "  - IAM split between Director and Mason service accounts" >&2
echo "  - time skew between host-side preflight and pool-side build execution" >&2
echo "" >&2
echo "Remediation: re-inscribe reliquary, re-yoke vessel, re-ordain hallmark." >&2
exit 1

content.text: Now add the helper function alongside about/vouch.

input.old_string:
######################################################################
# GAR Package Enumeration (Registry API — shared by tally, rekon, audit, batch_vouch)
input.new_string:
# Internal: assemble single-step preflight JSON array file
# Args: output_file temp_prefix
# Reads ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR and z_rbfc_tool_alpine from module state.
# Single step prepends to every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure, bind,
# graft, enshrine) as defense-in-depth: validates reliquary GAR-presence from
# the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step() {
  zrbfc_sentinel

  local -r z_output_file="$1"
  local -r z_temp_prefix="$2"

  local -r z_pscript_path="${ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR}/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh"
  local -r z_pbody_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_body.txt"
  local -r z_pescaped_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_escaped.txt"
  local -r z_psteps_file="${z_temp_prefix}preflight_steps.json"

  test -f "${z_pscript_path}" || buc_die "Preflight step script not found: ${z_pscript_path}"

  buc_log_args "Reading preflight step script (skip shebang)"
  tail -n +2 "${z_pscript_path}" > "${z_pbody_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to read preflight step script: ${z_pscript_path}"
  local z_pbody=""
  z_pbody=$(<"${z_pbody_file}")
  test -n "${z_pbody}" || buc_die "Empty preflight script body"

  printf '#!/bin/bash\n%s' "${z_pbody}" > "${z_pescaped_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to write escaped preflight script body"

  echo "[]" > "${z_output_file}" || buc_die "Failed to initialize preflight steps JSON"
  jq \
    --arg name "${z_rbfc_tool_alpine}" \
    --arg id "reliquary-preflight" \
    --rawfile script "${z_pescaped_file}" \
    '. + [{name: $name, id: $id, script: $script}]' \
    "${z_output_file}" > "${z_psteps_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to build preflight step JSON"
  mv "${z_psteps_file}" "${z_output_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to finalize preflight step JSON"
}

# GAR Package Enumeration (Registry API — shared by tally, rekon, audit, batch_vouch)

  local -r z_combined_steps="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"

input.new_string:
# === Assemble vouch steps ===
  local -r z_vouch_steps_file="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}vouch_steps.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps "${z_vouch_steps_file}" "${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}vouch_"

  # === Step 0: in-pool reliquary preflight (defense-in-depth) ===
  local -r z_preflight_step_file="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}preflight_step.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step "${z_preflight_step_file}" "${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}"

  # === Combine: preflight + about steps + vouch steps ===
  local -r z_combined_steps="${ZRBFV_GRAFT_META_PREFIX}combined_steps.json"

    > "${z_combined_steps}" || buc_die "Failed to combine preflight, about, and vouch steps"

toolUseResult.oldString:
# Resolve base images: ANCHOR (locator) → full GAR reference, or pass ORIGIN through.
  # Spec: RBSAC step "Resolve Base Images"
  # The locator carries its own namespace path (e.g. rbi_es/<anchor>:<anchor>);
  # paths within a GAR repo are prefix-free per the wrest/jettison convention.
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  local z_image_ref_1="" z_image_ref_2="" z_image_ref_3=""
  local z_ri_n="" z_ri_origin_var="" z_ri_anchor_var="" z_ri_origin="" z_ri_anchor=""
  local z_ri_pkg_path=""
  local z_ri_tag=""
  for z_ri_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_ri_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_ri_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_ri_origin="${!z_ri_origin_var:-}"
    z_ri_anchor="${!z_ri_anchor_var:-}"
    test -n "${z_ri_origin}" || continue
    local z_ri_ref=""
    if test -n "${z_ri_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_ri_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_ri_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_ri_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_ri_pkg_path="${z_ri_anchor%:*}"
      z_ri_tag="${z_ri_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_ri_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_ri_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
      z_ri_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_ri_pkg_path}:${z_ri_tag}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (anchored): ${z_ri_ref}"
    else
      z_ri_ref="${z_ri_origin}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (pass-through): ${z_ri_ref}"
    fi
    case "${z_ri_n}" in
      1) z_image_ref_1="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 2) z_image_ref_2="${z_ri_ref}" ;; 3) z_image_ref_3="${z_ri_ref}" ;;
    esac
  done
toolUseResult.newString:
# Resolve base images: ANCHOR (locator) → full GAR reference, or pass ORIGIN through.
  # Spec: RBSAC step "Resolve Base Images"
  # The locator carries its own namespace path (e.g. rbi_es/<anchor>:<anchor>);
  # paths within a GAR repo are prefix-free per the wrest/jettison convention.
  # Locator captures (z_image_locator_n) feed _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n substitutions
  # for the in-pool preflight step — anchored slots get HEAD-checked, pass-through
  # slots stay empty (preflight cannot reach upstream from the worker pool).
  local -r z_gar_repo_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}"

  local z_ri_pkg_path=""
  local z_ri_tag=""
  for z_ri_n in 1 2 3; do
    z_ri_origin_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ORIGIN"
    z_ri_anchor_var="RBRV_IMAGE_${z_ri_n}_ANCHOR"
    z_ri_origin="${!z_ri_origin_var:-}"

    local z_ri_ref=""
    local z_ri_locator=""
    if test -n "${z_ri_anchor}"; then
      case "${z_ri_anchor}" in
        *:*) : ;;
        *)   buc_die "Invalid ${z_ri_anchor_var} locator format (expected package-path:tag): ${z_ri_anchor}" ;;
      esac
      z_ri_pkg_path="${z_ri_anchor%:*}"
      z_ri_tag="${z_ri_anchor##*:}"
      test -n "${z_ri_pkg_path}" || buc_die "Package path is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
      test -n "${z_ri_tag}"      || buc_die "Tag is empty in ${z_ri_anchor_var}: ${z_ri_anchor}"
      z_ri_ref="${z_gar_repo_base}/${z_ri_pkg_path}:${z_ri_tag}"
      z_ri_locator="${z_ri_anchor}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (anchored): ${z_ri_ref}"
    else
      z_ri_ref="${z_ri_origin}"
      buc_log_args "Image slot ${z_ri_n} (pass-through): ${z_ri_ref}"

input.old_string:
# Combine: [extract-context] + image steps + about steps
  local -r z_all_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}all_steps.json"

    > "${z_all_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to prepend context extraction step"

# Step 0: in-pool reliquary preflight (defense-in-depth)
  local -r z_preflight_step_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}preflight_step.json"
  zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step "${z_preflight_step_file}" "${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}"

  # Combine: [preflight] + [extract-context] + image steps + about steps
  local -r z_all_steps_file="${ZRBFD_STITCH_PREFIX}all_steps.json"
  jq -s '.[0] + .[1] + .[2]' \
    "${z_preflight_step_file}" \
    <(jq -s '.' "${z_extract_step_file}") \
    "${z_accumulator_file}" \
    > "${z_all_steps_file}" || buc_die "Failed to combine preflight, context extraction, and image+about steps"

file.content:
# shellcheck disable=SC2016
  local -r z_cb_build_id='$BUILD_ID'

  # Pool routing: conjure/bind use vessel's egress mode
  local z_conjure_pool=""
  case "${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" in
    tether) z_conjure_pool="${RBDC_POOL_TETHER}" ;;
    airgap) z_conjure_pool="${RBDC_POOL_AIRGAP}" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRV_EGRESS_MODE: ${RBRV_EGRESS_MODE}" ;;
  esac

  jq -n \
    --slurpfile zjq_steps  "${z_all_steps_file}" \
    --slurpfile zjq_images "${z_images_file}" \
    --arg zjq_dockerfile        "${z_dockerfile_name}" \
    --arg zjq_vessel            "${z_sigil}" \
    --arg zjq_platforms         "${z_platforms}" \
    --arg zjq_platform_suffixes "${z_platform_suffixes_csv}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_location      "${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_project       "${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_repository    "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" \
    --arg zjq_git_commit        "${z_git_commit}" \
    --arg zjq_git_branch        "${z_git_branch}" \
    --arg zjq_git_repo          "${z_git_repo}" \
    --arg zjq_gar_host_suffix   "${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_hallmarks_root    "${RBGL_HALLMARKS_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_hallmark          "${z_hallmark}" \
    --arg zjq_inscribe_ts       "${z_hallmark%%-r*}" \
    --arg zjq_pool              "${z_conjure_pool}" \
    --arg zjq_timeout           "${RBRR_GCB_TIMEOUT}" \
    --arg zjq_mason_sa          "${z_mason_sa}" \
    --arg zjq_cb_build_id       "${z_cb_build_id}" \
    --arg zjq_rbga_gar_host     "${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}" \
    --arg zjq_rbga_gar_path     "${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}" \
    --arg zjq_rbga_dockerfile   "${z_stitch_dockerfile_content}" \
    --arg zjq_image_1           "${z_image_ref_1}" \
    --arg zjq_image_2           "${z_image_ref_2}" \
    --arg zjq_image_3           "${z_image_ref_3}" \
    --arg zjq_basename_image    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE}" \
    --arg zjq_basename_about    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT}" \
    --arg zjq_basename_attest   "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST}" \
    --arg zjq_basename_diags    "${RBGC_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS}" \
    --arg zjq_reliquaries_root  "${RBGL_RELIQUARIES_ROOT}" \
    --arg zjq_reliquary         "${RBRV_RELIQUARY}" \
    --arg zjq_locator_1         "${z_image_locator_1}" \
    --arg zjq_locator_2         "${z_image_locator_2}" \
    --arg zjq_locator_3         "${z_image_locator_3}" \
    '{
      steps: [$zjq_steps[0][] |
        if .args then
          .args = [.args[] | gsub("\\$\\{_RBGA_BUILD_ID\\}"; $zjq_cb_build_id) | gsub("\\$\\{_RBGA_BUILD_ID:-\\}"; $zjq_cb_build_id)]
        elif .script then
          .script = (.script | gsub("\\$\\{_RBGA_BUILD_ID\\}"; $zjq_cb_build_id) | gsub("\\$\\{_RBGA_BUILD_ID:-\\}"; $zjq_cb_build_id))
        else . end],
      images: $zjq_images[0],
      substitutions: {
        _RBGY_DOCKERFILE:          $zjq_dockerfile,
        _RBGY_PLATFORMS:           $zjq_platforms,
        _RBGY_PLATFORM_SUFFIXES:   $zjq_platform_suffixes,
        _RBGY_GAR_LOCATION:        $zjq_gar_location,
        _RBGY_GAR_PROJECT:         $zjq_gar_project,
        _RBGY_GAR_REPOSITORY:      $zjq_gar_repository,
        _RBGY_GIT_COMMIT:          $zjq_git_commit,
        _RBGY_GIT_BRANCH:          $zjq_git_branch,
        _RBGY_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX:     $zjq_gar_host_suffix,
        _RBGY_HALLMARKS_ROOT:      $zjq_hallmarks_root,
        _RBGY_HALLMARK:            $zjq_hallmark,
        _RBGY_IMAGE_1:             $zjq_image_1,
        _RBGY_IMAGE_2:             $zjq_image_2,
        _RBGY_IMAGE_3:             $zjq_image_3,
        _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE:  $zjq_basename_image,
        _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST: $zjq_basename_attest,
        _RBGY_ARK_BASENAME_DIAGS:  $zjq_basename_diags,
        _RBGA_GAR_HOST:            $zjq_rbga_gar_host,
        _RBGA_GAR_PATH:            $zjq_rbga_gar_path,
        _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT:      $zjq_hallmarks_root,
        _RBGA_HALLMARK:            $zjq_hallmark,
        _RBGA_VESSEL:              $zjq_vessel,
        _RBGA_VESSEL_MODE:         "conjure",
        _RBGA_GIT_COMMIT:          $zjq_git_commit,

content.description: New file Tools/rbk/rbgjr/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh. Builder: alpine reliquary tool. Uses busybox wget for HEAD-checks. Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGR_RELIQUARY, _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_1/2/3 (empty for non-conjure). Hardcoded tool list: gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo (alpine self-excluded, validated implicitly by CB pull). Accumulates misses, fails with 'this pool can't see «TOOL»' structured message naming missing items.

content.description: In Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh, mirror zrbfc_assemble_about_steps/zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps. Outputs single-step JSON array with step name=alpine reliquary tool, id=reliquary-preflight, script=preflight-step body. Used by all four entry points.

content.subject: Wire preflight into conjure entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_stitch_build_json (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~642), extend the existing extract-context prepend to include preflight first: [preflight, extract-context, ...image+about]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions (with enshrine locators populated from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR slots).

content.subject: Wire preflight into bind entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_mirror_submit (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~1514), prepend preflight to the mirror+about combine: [preflight, mirror, ...about]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty (bind doesn't use them).

content.subject: Wire preflight into graft entry point
content.description: In zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh ~309), prepend preflight to about+vouch combine: [preflight, ...about, ...vouch]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty.

content.subject: Wire preflight into enshrine entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_enshrine_submit (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~905), build 2-step array: [preflight, enshrine]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty (enshrine creates them, doesn't validate them).

content.content:
1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation conjures an
5	{rbtga_ark_image}
6	in
7	{rbtgi_repository}
8	by building a container image from a vessel specification. The
9	{rbtr_director}
10	performs the build via
11	{gcb_service}
12	using direct `builds.create` API submission.
13	
14	NOTE: Conjure produces both the image and about artifacts in a single Cloud Build job submitted via `builds.create`.
15	The about steps (`rbgja01`-`rbgja04`) are embedded via stitch alongside the build steps.
16	The vouch artifact is created by the subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} pipeline stage (always a separate Cloud Build job).
17	
18	NOTE: The
19	{rbtga_hallmark}
20	is minted locally by the
21	{rbtr_director}
22	before submission.
23	The Director knows the complete artifact tag set before the build begins -- naming authority and verification authority are the same entity.
24	
25	NOTE: The conjure build job runs on the worker pool selected by the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`: tether pool for vessels needing public internet (e.g., `apt-get` in Dockerfile), airgap pool for vessels with all dependencies enshrined in GAR.
26	The subsequent {rbtgo_ark_vouch} always runs on the airgap pool regardless of vessel egress mode.
27	
28	NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.
29	
30	The conjure operation:
31	
32	//axhopt_typed_parameter
33	{rbtga_ark}
34	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
35	.. {rbbc_require}
36	vessel provided as first argument
37	.. {rbbc_require}
38	vessel contains conjuring configuration (RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE, RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT)
39	
40	//axhos_step
41	. *Registry Preflight*:
42	.. *Reliquary check*: HEAD request to `«RELIQUARY»/docker:latest` manifest in
43	{gar_service}.
44	{rbbc_require}
45	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run inscribe first.
46	The reliquary is a co-versioned set of builder tool images inscribed from upstream into private GAR. Each vessel declares its reliquary; multiple datestamped reliquaries can coexist.
47	.. *Enshrine check*: For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where both
48	{rbrv_image_origin}
49	and
50	{rbrv_image_anchor}
51	are set:
52	... Parse
53	{rbrv_image_anchor}
54	as a
55	{rbst_locator}
56	into package-path and tag
57	... HEAD request to `«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»` manifest in
58	{gar_service}
59	... {rbbc_require}
60	HTTP 200 — on 404, fail with directive to run {rbtgo_ark_enshrine} first.
61	Multiple vessels sharing the same anchor need only one enshrine.
62	.. Preflight prevents wasted
63	{gcb_service}
64	submission time on missing images, with copy-paste remediation commands
65	
66	//axhos_step
67	. *Resolve Base Images*:
68	.. For each indexed slot (n = 1, 2, 3) where
69	{rbrv_image_origin}
70	is set:
71	... If
72	{rbrv_image_anchor}
73	is present: parse the
74	{rbst_locator}
75	and resolve to full GAR image reference (`«GAR-REPO-BASE»/«LOCATOR-PATH»:«LOCATOR-TAG»`)
76	... If
77	{rbrv_image_anchor}
78	is absent: pass
79	{rbrv_image_origin}
80	through as the upstream tag (development path)

input.old_string: NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.
input.new_string:
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight*. Step 0 of the conjure Cloud Build job HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage, before extract-context and the image+about steps run. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo) plus the vessel's enshrined base anchors; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item; remediation is re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain. The host-side `Registry Preflight` step (below) verifies the Director's host-side view of GAR; the in-pool preflight catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.

15	service account on the private pool (`RBDC_GCB_POOL_STEM`).
16	
17	NOTE: The about steps receive their context via substitution variables (`_RBGA_*`) and workspace state, not via function parameters.
18	For conjure, the hallmark is read from `/workspace` (computed by the preceding build steps); for bind and graft, it comes from the `_RBGA_HALLMARK` substitution variable.

21	NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before about runs. The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.
22	
23	//axhos_step
24	. *Cloud Build Step 1: Discover Platforms* (Python 3):
25	.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
26	.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server
27	.. {rbbc_call}
28	Docker Registry API: GET manifests for `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/image:«HALLMARK»` with OCI media type accept headers
29	.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
30	... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
31	OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
32	Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
33	They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
34	Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
35	+
36	See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
37	... {rbbc_store}
38	`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
39	.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
40	... {rbbc_call}
41	GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
42	... {rbbc_store}
43	`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
44	.. {rbbc_store}

.. Image: gcloud (from reliquary)
.. Fetch OAuth token from GCE metadata server

.. If response is an OCI image index or Docker manifest list:
... Filter out attestation manifests before extracting platforms.
OCI image indexes may contain non-runnable attestation manifests (SLSA provenance, SBOMs) alongside real platform manifests.
Per the BuildKit attestation storage specification, these attestation manifests are deliberately assigned platform `unknown/unknown` (`"architecture": "unknown", "os": "unknown"`) to prevent container runtimes from pulling them as images.
They carry media types such as `application/vnd.in-toto+json` and are annotated with `"vnd.docker.reference.type": "attestation-manifest"`.
Exclude any manifest entry where _both_ `platform.os` and `platform.architecture` are `unknown`.
+
See: https://docs.docker.com/build/metadata/attestations/attestation-storage/[Docker Attestation Storage], https://github.com/moby/buildkit/blob/master/docs/attestations/attestation-storage.md[BuildKit Attestation Storage spec].
... {rbbc_store}
`«PLATFORMS»` as list of platform descriptors from the remaining (non-attestation) manifest entries
.. If response is a single OCI/Docker manifest (typical for bind and graft):
... {rbbc_call}
GET config blob via digest from manifest's `config.digest` field
... {rbbc_store}
`«PLATFORMS»` as single-element list from config's `os` and `architecture` fields
.. {rbbc_store}
