1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
5	{gar_service}
6	as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
7	The
8	{rbtr_director}
9	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each declared
10	{rbrv_image_origin}
11	from its upstream registry to GAR using
12	the reliquary skopeo image
13	(`skopeo copy --all`), preserving the manifest list and all per-platform manifests for multi-platform images.
14	The locator results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15	{rbrv_image_anchor}
16	is then written back to the vessel regime locally as a
17	{rbst_locator}.
18	
19	NOTE: Slots whose
20	{rbrv_image_anchor}
21	is already populated are processed under skip-if-set semantics.
22	The Director HEAD-validates that the locator resolves in
23	{gar_service}
24	and leaves the slot's
25	{rbrv_image_anchor}
26	untouched — no Cloud Build copy occurs for that slot.
27	This admits hallmark-base vessels: a vessel whose base image is an internal hallmark (e.g., the airgap-bottle building FROM another vessel's just-ordained hallmark) carries an
28	{rbrv_image_anchor}
29	of `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»` written by the orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) before enshrine runs; enshrine then verifies the hallmark is reachable rather than re-mirroring it.
30	A vessel whose every slot is pre-populated requires no Cloud Build submission — enshrine reduces to local HEAD validation.
31	
32	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
33	It must be run before
34	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
35	when anchored base images are desired.
36	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
37	
38	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
39	No local skopeo installation is required.
40	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
41

43

45	{rbrv_sigil}

47	.. {rbbc_require}
48	vessel provided as first argument
49	.. {rbbc_require}
50	vessel declares at least one base-image slot — that is, at least one of slots 1-3 has either

content.subject: Create rbgjr01 preflight step script
content.description: New file Tools/rbk/rbgjr/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh. Builder: alpine reliquary tool. Uses busybox wget for HEAD-checks. Substitutions: _RBGR_GAR_HOST, _RBGR_GAR_PATH, _RBGR_RELIQUARIES_ROOT, _RBGR_RELIQUARY, _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_1/2/3 (empty for non-conjure). Hardcoded tool list: gcloud docker syft binfmt skopeo (alpine self-excluded, validated implicitly by CB pull). Accumulates misses, fails with 'this pool can't see «TOOL»' structured message naming missing items.

content.subject: Add zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step helper
content.description: In Tools/rbk/rbfc_FoundryCore.sh, mirror zrbfc_assemble_about_steps/zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps. Outputs single-step JSON array with step name=alpine reliquary tool, id=reliquary-preflight, script=preflight-step body. Used by all four entry points.

content.subject: Wire preflight into conjure entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_stitch_build_json (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~642), extend the existing extract-context prepend to include preflight first: [preflight, extract-context, ...image+about]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions (with enshrine locators populated from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR slots).

content.subject: Wire preflight into bind entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_mirror_submit (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~1514), prepend preflight to the mirror+about combine: [preflight, mirror, ...about]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty (bind doesn't use them).

content.subject: Wire preflight into graft entry point
content.description: In zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit (rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh ~309), prepend preflight to about+vouch combine: [preflight, ...about, ...vouch]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty.

content.subject: Wire preflight into enshrine entry point
content.description: In zrbfd_enshrine_submit (rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh ~905), build 2-step array: [preflight, enshrine]. Add _RBGR_* substitutions; enshrine locators empty (enshrine creates them, doesn't validate them).

content.subject: Spec sweep — RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE, RBS0
content.description: RBSAC (conjure), RBSAB (about — for combined-about contexts in conjure/bind), RBSAV (vouch — for combined about+vouch in graft), RBSAE (enshrine): declare in-pool preflight as step 0. RBS0:800 paragraph: extend to mention in-pool preflight contract per docket. Document chicken-and-egg boundary (alpine self-validation by CB pull).

content.subject: Verify build + fast test suite
content.description: tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh green; tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh maintains BBAA4 baseline. Empirical wrap-criterion runs (preflight fires green + injected miss) deferred to BBAAs per established precedent (BBAA4 deferred similarly).

input.old_string:
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
input.new_string:
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight contract*. Every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, enshrine) prepends a step 0 — the in-pool reliquary preflight (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) — that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage. This is defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR): the in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain). Standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered by the in-pool preflight.

toolUseResult.newString:
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight contract*. Every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, enshrine) prepends a step 0 — the in-pool reliquary preflight (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) — that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage. This is defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR): the in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain). Standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered by the in-pool preflight.

content.text:
All work landed. Pace BBAA3 ready to wrap.

## Summary

**Code surfaces:**
- `Tools/rbk/rbgjr/rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh` — new step script. Alpine + busybox-wget HEAD-checks 5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo) plus enshrine slot locators. Alpine self-validated by CB pull. Structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" failure message naming each miss.
- `rbfc_FoundryCore.sh` — `zrbfc_assemble_preflight_step` helper (mirrors about/vouch helpers), `ZRBFC_RBGJR_STEPS_DIR` registered in kindle.
- `rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh` — preflight prepended in conjure stitch (extends 2-way combine to 3-way: preflight + extract-context + image+about), bind submit (3-way: preflight + mirror + about), enshrine submit (2-way: preflight + enshrine). Conjure passes enshrine locators from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`; bind/enshrine pass empty.
- `rbfv_FoundryVerify.sh` — preflight prepended in graft about+vouch combine (3-way: preflight + about + vouch).

**Spec sweep:** RBSAC, RBSAB, RBSAV, RBSAE each gained an "In-pool reliquary preflight" NOTE with mode-specific scope. RBS0:800 paragraph extended with the contract — defense-in-depth, alpine self-validation chicken-and-egg boundary, and explicit out-of-scope note for standalone vouch/about (operator-recovery paths).

**Verification:**
- `bash -n` green on all 4 modified shell files + new step script
- `tt/rbtd-b.Build.sh` green (theurge unchanged)
- `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` baseline match: 47/47 enrollment-validation + 25/26 regime-validation; pre-existing `rbtdrf_rv_rbrv_all_vessels` fail unchanged from BBAA4 baseline (marshal-zero validator surfacing)
- Tabtarget context regen produced no diff (no new tabtargets — preflight is internal)

**Empirical wrap-criterion (deferred to BBAAs):** Per established BBAA4 precedent. Pending against canonical-established depot:
- Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path CB job
- Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) produces clean structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message
- Green case overhead ~5s

**Out-of-scope per docket as mounted:** standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) — surfaced during study but accepted as not part of this pace's surface; user explicitly chose "mount as written" over reslate.

content.content:
782	
783	== Key Premises
784	
785	[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
786	//axl_voices axk_premise
787	{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
788	{at_recipe_bottle}
789	scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
790	All local
791	{gcp_service}
792	interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
793	+
794	**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
795	This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
796	All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
797	The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
798	Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
799	+
800	In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
801	+
802	Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
803	The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
804	+
805	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
806	The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
807	
808	NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight contract*. Every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, enshrine) prepends a step 0 — the in-pool reliquary preflight (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) — that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage. This is defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR): the in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain). Standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered by the in-pool preflight.
809	
810	[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
811	//axl_voices axk_premise
812	{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
813	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
814	The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
815	Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
816	Batched invest is not supported — by design.

file.content:
== Key Premises

[[rbsk_no_gcloud]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_no_gcloud}::
{at_recipe_bottle}
scripts MUST NOT invoke the `gcloud` CLI in local bash scripts or transitively through helper scripts.
All local
{gcp_service}
interaction uses direct REST API calls via `curl` with JWT-authenticated bearer tokens.
+
**Exception — vouch provenance fetch**: The {rbtgo_ark_vouch} verification step (conjure mode only) invokes `gcloud artifacts docker images describe --show-provenance` for SLSA provenance envelope retrieval.
This wraps complex Container Analysis API interaction that would be burdensome to replicate via raw REST.
All other GCB step operations use the GCE metadata server for OAuth token acquisition and direct registry REST API calls — no gcloud.
The provenance fetch runs as Python 3 on the gcloud builder image after the primary image is built; the reproducibility concern that motivates this premise is weaker for post-build verification.
Works under NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS (Private Google Access reaches Google APIs).
+
In GCB build step contexts (conjure, bind, graft, enshrine), registry authentication uses the GCE metadata server to obtain an access token and writes a static Docker config JSON.
+
Rationale: `gcloud` is an opaque, version-sensitive binary that obscures API semantics, introduces implicit authentication state, and breaks reproducibility.
The `gcloud auth configure-docker` approach also fails mechanically in GCB steps — the gcloud builder image lacks `docker` in PATH, so the credential helper it installs cannot function.
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight contract*. Every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, enshrine) prepends a step 0 — the in-pool reliquary preflight (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) — that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage. This is defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR): the in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain). Standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered by the in-pool preflight.

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.

content.text: Now restructure the RBS0 NOTE into a proper premise.

input.old_string:
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight contract*. Every ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, enshrine) prepends a step 0 — the in-pool reliquary preflight (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) — that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage. This is defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR): the in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain). Standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered by the in-pool preflight.

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]

//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}::
Every ordain-path Cloud Build job — conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, and enshrine — prepends a step 0 (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
Conjure additionally HEAD-checks the vessel's enshrined base anchors (slots 1-3 from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`); other modes pass empty locator substitutions.
+
Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools — gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
Alpine, the preflight step's own image, is validated implicitly by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs.
Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain).
+
Rationale: defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR).
The in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap private-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
+
Scope boundary: standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered.

content.text: Now trim the four operation specs.

input.old_string:
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight*. Step 0 of the conjure Cloud Build job HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage, before extract-context and the image+about steps run. Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo) plus the vessel's enshrined base anchors; alpine (the preflight step's own image) is implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it. Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item; remediation is re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain. The host-side `Registry Preflight` step (below) verifies the Director's host-side view of GAR; the in-pool preflight catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
input.new_string:
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.

NOTE: The conjure Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the extract-context step. Conjure populates `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n` substitutions from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` slots, so the preflight HEAD-checks each anchored base in addition to the reliquary tools.

lastPrompt: notch first.  I see quite a lot of specification edits that are highly likely to create 'word cancer', where instead of threading the wording to follow DRY, you are simply copy/pasting lists in multip…

input.old_string:
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before about runs. The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.

NOTE: *In-pool reliquary preflight*. When about steps are embedded in an ordain-path Cloud Build job (conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch), step 0 of the parent job is the in-pool reliquary preflight that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images and (for conjure) enshrined base anchors from the worker pool's vantage before about steps run. The about steps themselves do not inject the preflight — it is prepended at the entry-point level (`zrbfd_stitch_build_json`, `zrbfd_mirror_submit`, `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`). Standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`, operator-recovery path) does not currently include the preflight.

The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.

NOTE: The {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} is prepended at the entry-point level (`zrbfd_stitch_build_json`, `zrbfd_mirror_submit`, `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`); the about-step assembly itself does not inject it.

content.content:
=== RBSAC NOTE ===
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before this operation runs. The about/vouch pipeline (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) consumes reliquary tool images uniformly across all modes; the registry preflight below enforces this.

NOTE: The conjure Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the extract-context step. Conjure populates `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n` substitutions from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` slots, so the preflight HEAD-checks each anchored base in addition to the reliquary tools.

The conjure operation:
=== RBSAB NOTE ===
NOTE: *Universal reliquary precondition*. Every ordain-path vessel — conjure, bind, and graft — must have `RBRV_RELIQUARY` non-empty before about runs. The four steps below execute on reliquary-sourced tool images (gcloud, docker, alpine, syft) regardless of mode; the parent operation's registry preflight enforces this before any Cloud Build submission.

NOTE: The {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} is prepended at the entry-point level (`zrbfd_stitch_build_json`, `zrbfd_mirror_submit`, `zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`); the about-step assembly itself does not inject it.

//axhos_step
=== RBSAV NOTE ===
NOTE: Vouch participates in the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} only when embedded in graft's combined about+vouch Cloud Build job (`zrbfv_graft_metadata_submit`). The standalone vouch submission (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`, the post-image stage of conjure and bind ordain flows) is out of scope per the premise.

The
=== RBSAE NOTE ===
NOTE: The enshrine Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the skopeo copy. Enshrine passes empty locator substitutions — enshrine's purpose is to create anchors, so pre-validating them would be circular.

The enshrine operation:

content.content:
805	+
806	NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
807	The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.
808	
809	[[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]]
810	//axl_voices axk_premise
811	{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}::
812	Every ordain-path Cloud Build job — conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, and enshrine — prepends a step 0 (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
813	Conjure additionally HEAD-checks the vessel's enshrined base anchors (slots 1-3 from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`); other modes pass empty locator substitutions.
814	+
815	Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools — gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
816	Alpine, the preflight step's own image, is validated implicitly by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs.
817	Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain).
818	+
819	Rationale: defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR).
820	The in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap private-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
821	+
822	Scope boundary: standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered.
823	
824	[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
825	//axl_voices axk_premise
826	{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
827	{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
828	The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
829	Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
830	Batched invest is not supported — by design.
831	+
832	Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.

file.content:
+
NOTE: The gcloud reliquary image exists because every ordain-path build step (conjure, bind, graft) uses it for the about/vouch pipeline (`bash`, `date`, and `gcloud` for provenance retrieval per the exception above).
The reliquary toolchain is mode-uniform: all ordain modes consume gcloud, docker, alpine, and syft from the reliquary; bind additionally uses skopeo for image-copy.

[[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}::
Every ordain-path Cloud Build job — conjure's combined image+about, bind's combined mirror+about, graft's combined about+vouch, and enshrine — prepends a step 0 (`rbgjr01-reliquary-preflight.sh`) that HEAD-checks reliquary tool images from the worker pool's vantage before expensive work runs.
Conjure additionally HEAD-checks the vessel's enshrined base anchors (slots 1-3 from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`); other modes pass empty locator substitutions.
+
Coverage is 5 of 6 reliquary tools — gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo.
Alpine, the preflight step's own image, is validated implicitly by Cloud Build pulling it before the script runs.
Failure produces a structured "this pool can't see «TOOL»" message naming each missed item, with remediation directive (re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain).
+
Rationale: defense-in-depth on top of the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` (which validates the Director's host-side view of GAR).
The in-pool check catches divergence the host-side check cannot — airgap private-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, and submit↔execute time skew.
+
Scope boundary: standalone vouch (`zrbfv_vouch_submit`) and standalone about (`zrbfv_about_submit`) are operator-recovery paths and are not currently covered.

[[rbsk_invest_serialized]]
//axl_voices axk_premise
{rbsk_invest_serialized}::
{rbtgo_retriever_invest}, {rbtgo_director_invest}, and {rbtgo_governor_mantle} write credential bytes to a singleton assay slot (`RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE`), not a per-identity slot.
The operator MUST move `RBDC_ASSAY_RBRA_FILE` to a safe place before initiating the next operation that writes to the assay slot — typically the role's production slot (`RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE`, `RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE`, or `RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE`) when the invester and the consumer are the same operator, otherwise a delivery channel to the consumer.
Two consecutive assay-slot writers without an intervening move lose the first writer's bytes.
Batched invest is not supported — by design.
+
Rationale: a singleton assay slot keeps the producer-output discipline uniform across roles and frees the implementation from path-space per-identity routing.

content.text:
DRY pass complete. Comparison:

**Before** (per-spec duplication):
- "in-pool reliquary preflight": 5x prose copies
- "5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo)": 3x
- "alpine implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it": 3x
- "structured 'this pool can't see «TOOL»' message": 3x
- "remediation is re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain": 3x
- Divergence sources list: 2x
- Scope-boundary list of standalone vouch/about: 2x

**After**: All shared facts live exactly once in the new `rbsk_in_pool_preflight` Key Premise in RBS0 (alongside `rbsk_no_gcloud` and `rbsk_invest_serialized`). The four operation specs carry only mode-specific facts and reference the premise via `{rbsk_in_pool_preflight}`:

| Spec | Mode-specific NOTE content |
|---|---|
| RBSAC | step 0 ahead of extract-context; `_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n` populated from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR` |
| RBSAB | preflight prepended at entry-point level, not in about-step assembly |
| RBSAV | participates only in graft's combined CB; standalone vouch out of scope per the premise |
| RBSAE | step 0 ahead of skopeo copy; empty locators (would be circular) |

**Mapping section**: added `:rbsk_in_pool_preflight: <<rbsk_in_pool_preflight,In-Pool Reliquary Preflight>>`. Cross-doc resolution works because RBS0 includes RBSAC/RBSAB/RBSAV/RBSAE.

Word-cancer audit revealed five facts copy-pasted 2-5x: 'in-pool reliquary preflight' itself (5x), '5 of 6 reliquary tools (gcloud, docker, syft, binfmt, skopeo)' (3x), 'alpine implicitly validated by Cloud Build pulling it' (3x), 'structured this pool can't see TOOL message' (3x), 'remediation re-inscribe + re-yoke + re-ordain' (3x), divergence sources list (airgap-VPC routing, Director↔Mason IAM split, submit↔execute time skew) (2x), full ordain-path contexts list (2x), scope-boundary list of standalone vouch/about (2x). Resolution: minted rbsk_in_pool_preflight as a third Key Premise sibling to rbsk_no_gcloud and rbsk_invest_serialized in RBS0 — anchor [[rbsk_in_pool_preflight]] + axl_voices axk_premise + structured definition with three '+' continuations (canonical operation, coverage details, rationale, scope boundary). Mapping section gained ':rbsk_in_pool_preflight: <<rbsk_in_pool_preflight,In-Pool Reliquary Preflight>>' alongside existing rbsk premises. Each operation-spec NOTE trimmed to mode-specific facts only with {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} reference: RBSAC retains 'step 0 ahead of extract-context, _RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n populated from RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR' (the conjure-only locator population is genuinely mode-specific); RBSAB retains 'preflight prepended at entry-point level not in about-step assembly' (the assembly-vs-entry-point distinction is structural detail relevant only to about); RBSAV retains 'participates only in graft's combined CB; standalone vouch out of scope per the premise' (the embed-vs-standalone distinction is vouch-specific); RBSAE retains 'step 0 ahead of skopeo copy; empty locators — enshrine creates anchors so pre-validating circular' (the circularity argument is enshrine-specific). Cross-doc resolution works because RBS0 includes all four operation specs (line 1267, 1285, 1313, 1334). Net effect: +19/-5 lines across 5 specs — the NOTE trim per file averages -4 lines; RBS0 gains the new premise (~14 lines) but the three '+' continuations replace what was a single dense paragraph plus four duplications elsewhere. content.text:
Both notches landed (initial + DRY). 

**Items worth flagging before wrap:**

1. **Adjacencies surfaced but not fixed** (acknowledged in the spec's premise scope boundary):
   - `zrbfv_vouch_submit` (standalone vouch) — reached during conjure/bind ordain flows after the image+about CB completes; pulls reliquary tools but has no in-pool preflight
   - `zrbfv_about_submit` (standalone about) — operator-recovery path; same gap
   - Both ALSO lack the host-side `zrbfd_registry_preflight` call (parallel to what BBAA4 plugged for the four ordain entry points)

   These are explicit out-of-scope items in `rbsk_in_pool_preflight`'s "Scope boundary" continuation. A follow-up pace could extend coverage; I'd recommend it land as one notch since the changes are mechanical (call the same helper at the same prepend point) and the host-side fix is a one-line add to `rbfv_vouch`. Worth minting an itch if not slated immediately.

2. **Wrap criteria status:**
   - ✅ Preflight runs as step 0 in every ordain-path CB job — verifiable by inspection of generated build JSON; helper called from all four submit functions, jq combine extends each step array
   - ⏸ Injected miss (post-jettison rerun) → "this pool can't see «TOOL»" — empirical, deferred to BBAAs
   - ⏸ Green case overhead ~5s — empirical, deferred to BBAAs

   The BBAA4 precedent (deferring empirical to BBAAs) applies cleanly here.

3. **Things I'm satisfied about:**
   - Step script shellcheck-clean, follows the rbgja/rbgje/rbgjm/rbgji conventions (header docblock, env-var checks, metadata-server token, structured FATAL exits)
   - Helper mirrors `zrbfc_assemble_about_steps`/`zrbfc_assemble_vouch_steps` shape exactly
   - Substitution wiring uniform across all four entry points; conjure adds genuine variance (`_RBGR_ENSHRINE_LOCATOR_n` populated from `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`)
   - Premise is now the single source of truth; operation specs read as cross-references not copies
   - Build green; fast suite at BBAA4 baseline

4. **Nothing else to do code-wise.** The pace's docket surface is fully covered; the empirical part is the one deferred-by-precedent piece.

Ready to wrap ₢BBAA3?

1	//axhob_operation
2	{rbtgo_ark_enshrine}
3	
4	NOTE: This operation enshrines upstream base images into
5	{gar_service}
6	as content-addressed, human-readable anchors.
7	The
8	{rbtr_director}
9	submits a Cloud Build job that copies each declared
10	{rbrv_image_origin}
11	from its upstream registry to GAR using
12	the reliquary skopeo image
13	(`skopeo copy --all`), preserving the manifest list and all per-platform manifests for multi-platform images.
14	The locator results are returned via `buildStepOutputs` and
15	{rbrv_image_anchor}
16	is then written back to the vessel regime locally as a
17	{rbst_locator}.
18	
19	NOTE: This operation is a pure mirror.
20	Every call inspects current upstream for each declared
21	{rbrv_image_origin}
22	, computes the content-addressed locator from the live manifest digest, copies the image into
23	{gar_service}
24	, and writes the resulting
25	{rbst_locator}
26	into the vessel's
27	{rbrv_image_anchor}
28	— overwriting whatever was previously committed there.
29	The
30	{rbrv_image_anchor}
31	is a derived artifact of this operation, not an operator-set pin.
32	Re-runs are idempotent with respect to upstream state, not with respect to the file's prior contents — when upstream rotates, the next call writes a different locator and that is the operation surfacing the change, not a fault.
33	
34	NOTE: Vessels whose
35	{rbrv_image_anchor}
36	is written by an upstream procedure as a hallmark-pin (e.g., the airgap-bottle pointing at the just-ordained forge image via `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/«BASENAME»:«HALLMARK»`) do not invoke this operation.
37	The orchestrating procedure (handbook step or onboarding-sequence fixture) writes the locator directly;
38	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
39	resolves it at build time.
40	The hallmark's existence in
41	{gar_service}
42	is established by the producer's
43	{rbtgo_ark_ordain}
44	success, not by a separate validation step here.
45	
46	NOTE: Enshrine is a pre-build preparation step.
47	It must be run before
48	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
49	when anchored base images are desired.
50	Conjure works without anchors (passing ORIGIN through), but anchored images provide reproducibility and air-gap readiness.
51	
52	NOTE: Enshrine runs on Cloud Build, not the Director's workstation.
53	No local skopeo installation is required.
54	The GCB step uses Mason SA ambient credentials via the metadata server for GAR writes.
55	
56	NOTE: The enshrine Cloud Build job carries the {rbsk_in_pool_preflight} as its step 0, ahead of the skopeo copy. Enshrine passes empty locator substitutions — enshrine's purpose is to create anchors, so pre-validating them would be circular.
57	
58	The enshrine operation:
59	
60	//axhopt_typed_parameter
61	{rbrv_sigil}
62	. *Validate Input Parameters*:
63	.. {rbbc_require}
64	vessel provided as first argument
65	.. {rbbc_require}
66	vessel declares at least one upstream base-image slot — that is, at least one of slots 1-3 has
67	{rbrv_image_origin}
68	populated.
69	{rbrv_image_anchor}
70	is computed by this operation; an ANCHOR-only slot is not a valid input shape
71	.. {rbbc_require}
72	vessel mode is `conjure` (`RBRV_VESSEL_MODE`)
73	.. {rbbc_require}
74	reliquary tool images exist in GAR (HEAD check on `docker:latest` canary)
75	
76	//axhos_step
77	. *Submit Enshrine Cloud Build Job*:
78	.. Compose `builds.create` JSON with:
79	... Single step using
80	the reliquary skopeo image
81	as the builder image (script: `rbgje01-enshrine-copy.sh`)
82	... `_RBGE_*` substitutions: `_RBGE_GAR_HOST`, `_RBGE_GAR_PATH`, `_RBGE_IMAGE_n_ORIGIN` (n = 1, 2, 3; empty for unused slots). Base images are stored in the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace, with each anchor as a peer package (`{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»`), not per-vessel.
83	... Mason SA, private pool, timeout
84	.. Submit via `builds.create` and wait for completion
85	.. The GCB step, for each non-empty ORIGIN slot:
86	... Fetches the raw upstream manifest (`skopeo inspect --raw`) and computes its sha256 digest (`sha256sum` of the raw manifest bytes)
87	... {rbbc_store}
88	`<<DIGEST>>` -- the sha256 digest of the upstream manifest (manifest list digest for multi-platform images, single manifest digest for single-platform images)
89	... Constructs anchor: sanitize full origin string (`:` `/` replaced with `-`), append first 10 hex chars of sha256 digest
90	... Example: `python:3.11-slim` with `sha256:abc123def4...` yields anchor `python-3.11-slim-abc123def4`
91	... Composes the
92	{rbst_locator}

94	... Copies upstream image into the shared `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/` namespace in GAR as a peer package at `{gar_enshrines_namespace}/«ANCHOR»:«ANCHOR»` using `skopeo copy --all`, transferring the manifest list and all per-platform manifests atomically. Each anchor is its own package under the namespace — the path segment carries the anchor identity, and the tag redundantly restates it so divergence is a visible bug. The anchor is vessel-independent — multiple vessels sharing the same base image origin share one enshrined package.
95	... {rbbc_require}
96	copy succeeds -- the locator now resolves to a permanent, content-addressed reference in
97	{gar_service}
98	containing all upstream platforms
99	... Single-platform upstream images are handled identically -- `skopeo copy --all` is a no-op on the platform dimension when only one platform exists
100	.. Writes JSON locator results to `/builder/outputs/output` (returned as `buildStepOutputs`); each slot's `anchor` field carries the full locator string
101

105	.. For each slot present in the result:

107	
108	//axhoot_typed_output
109	{rbrv_image_anchor}
110	Per-slot locator string written to the vessel regime.
111

113	Successful execution produces anchored base image references in
114	{gar_service}
115	(with all upstream platforms preserved) and updates the vessel regime with
116	{rbrv_image_anchor}
117	values.
118	The vessel is now ready for reproducible
119	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
120	builds using GAR-mirrored base images.
121	Multi-platform conjure vessels pull the correct platform from the anchored manifest list automatically.
122	Re-running enshrine with the same
123	{rbrv_image_origin}
124	but a different upstream digest produces a new locator (upstream image was updated).
